sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for all pilots from the United States.

sql: SELECT Model, Order_Year FROM aircraft WHERE Manufacturer = 'Gillig';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the model and order year for all aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Team FROM pilot WHERE Rank = 6;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and team of any pilot with a rank of 6.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Position FROM pilot WHERE Join_Year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and position for any pilot who joined in the year 2009.

sql: SELECT Manufacturer, Model FROM aircraft WHERE Fuel_Propulsion = 'Diesel';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the manufacturer and model for all aircraft that use diesel for fuel propulsion.

sql: SELECT * FROM pilot_record WHERE Date = '2003/01/04';
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the complete pilot record, including pilot and aircraft IDs, for the specific date of January 4th, 2003.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age FROM pilot ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each pilot's name and age, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT Model, Order_Year, Manufacturer FROM aircraft ORDER BY Order_Year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each aircraft's model, manufacturer, and order year, sorted chronologically from the earliest order to the most recent.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Position = 'Center Team';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the name and age of each pilot assigned to the 'Center Team' position.

sql: SELECT * FROM aircraft WHERE Order_Year = 1996;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for aircraft ordered in the year 1996.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T2.Date FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each pilot's name and the date for every corresponding pilot record they have.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(Pilot_ID) FROM pilot GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each nationality along with the total count of pilots from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each pilot's name along with the total count of their associated records.

sql: SELECT Position, AVG(Age) FROM pilot GROUP BY Position;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique pilot position and the average age of the pilots holding that position.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T3.Model, T3.Manufacturer FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each pilot from the United States, along with the model and manufacturer for each aircraft they have a flight record for.

sql: SELECT Order_Year, COUNT(Aircraft_ID) FROM aircraft GROUP BY Order_Year;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each order year alongside the total count of aircraft ordered in that year.

sql: SELECT Team, COUNT(Pilot_ID) FROM pilot GROUP BY Team;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each team along with the total count of pilots on that team.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T2.Date = '2004/01/04';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the pilot who had a record on the specific date of January 4, 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Model;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each aircraft model alongside a total count of the pilot records associated with that model.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T3.Model, T3.Manufacturer FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Position = 'Center Team';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name of each pilot in the 'Center Team' position, along with the model and manufacturer of the aircraft they have flown.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_ID HAVING count(T2.Record_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any pilot who has more than one flight record.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Rank = (SELECT MAX(Rank) FROM pilot);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of the pilot who holds the highest rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Aircraft_ID HAVING count(DISTINCT T2.Pilot_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the aircraft model that has been flown by two or more different pilots.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age, Nationality FROM pilot WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM pilot);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, age, and nationality of pilots who are older than the average age of all pilots.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, T1.Manufacturer, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Aircraft_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Record_ID) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the top three aircraft models and their manufacturers based on the number of pilot records, ordered from most to fewest records.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, T1.Manufacturer FROM aircraft AS T1 LEFT JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Record_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the model and manufacturer of aircraft that have no corresponding pilot records.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' AND T1.Pilot_ID IN (SELECT T2.Pilot_ID FROM pilot_record AS T2 JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of pilots from the United States who have flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, T1.Order_Year FROM aircraft AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pilot_record AS T2 JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T2.Aircraft_ID = T1.Aircraft_ID AND T3.Nationality = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the model and order year for each aircraft that has been operated by at least one pilot with United States nationality.

sql: SELECT Team, AVG(Age) AS average_age FROM pilot GROUP BY Team ORDER BY average_age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single pilot team with the highest average pilot age.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Order_Year = 1996 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Order_Year = 1998;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Order_Year = 1992;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a pilot who has flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig' but has not flown any aircraft ordered in 1992.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Rank, T1.Team FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Rank > (SELECT AVG(T2.Rank) FROM pilot AS T2 WHERE T2.Team = T1.Team);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Model) AS num_models_flown FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name ORDER BY num_models_flown DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each pilot's name and the total number of distinct aircraft models they have flown, ordered from the highest count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Manufacturer, T3.Model FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM pilot);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the unique manufacturer and model of the aircraft that has been flown by the youngest pilot(s) on record.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS flight_count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name HAVING COUNT(T2.Record_ID) > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Record_ID) AS c FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID));
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying pilots who have flown more times than the average, showing each qualifying pilot's name and their total flight count.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Join_Year FROM pilot WHERE Join_Year = (SELECT Join_Year FROM pilot WHERE Pilot_name = 'Patrick O''Bryant') AND Pilot_name <> 'Patrick O''Bryant';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T2.Pilot_name, T1.Nationality, T1.Team FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team AND T1.Nationality = T2.Nationality WHERE T1.Pilot_ID < T2.Pilot_ID;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Manufacturer, AVG(T1.Age) AS avg_pilot_age FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T3.Manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each aircraft manufacturer along with the average age of the pilots who have flown their aircraft.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age, Rank FROM pilot WHERE Age > 35 UNION SELECT Pilot_name, Age, Rank FROM pilot WHERE Rank < 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the name, age, and rank for any pilot who is either older than 35 or has a rank less than 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T3.Fuel_Propulsion <> 'Diesel' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of pilots who have exclusively flown aircraft powered by Diesel fuel.

sql: SELECT * FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing a complete list of all recorded races with their corresponding ID, name, class, date, and track ID.

sql: SELECT * FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a complete list of all race tracks with details on their name, location, seating capacity, and the year they opened.

sql: SELECT * FROM race WHERE Name = 'Rolex 24 At Daytona';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with all available details for the specific race named 'Rolex 24 At Daytona'.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Location = 'Fontana, CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and seating capacity for any racetrack located in 'Fontana, CA'.

sql: SELECT Name, Year_Opened FROM track ORDER BY Year_Opened DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each track's name and the year it opened, ordered from the most recently opened to the oldest.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all races.

sql: SELECT avg(Seating) FROM track;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average seating capacity across all tracks.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Class FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique race class.

sql: SELECT Name, Year_Opened FROM track WHERE Year_Opened >= 2000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and opening year of all race tracks that were established in or after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM race WHERE Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and date for all races belonging to the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows that pair each race name with the name of the track where it takes place.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Auto Club Speedway';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the race held at the 'Auto Club Speedway' track.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, count(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each race track by name along with the total count of races held at that track.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the track that has hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all tracks that have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT avg(T2.Seating) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average seating capacity for all tracks that host 'DP/GT' class races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating > 80000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of races that take place at high-capacity tracks with seating for more than 80,000 people.

sql: SELECT T2.Location FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Mexico City 250';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the location of the track where the 'Mexico City 250' race is held.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, count(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY count(T1.Race_ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows ranking race tracks by the total number of races they have hosted, ordered from the track with the most races to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID ORDER BY T2.Location ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each race and its corresponding track location, ordered alphabetically by location.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating > (SELECT avg(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and seating capacity for tracks that have an above-average seating capacity.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating = (SELECT max(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and seating capacity of the racetrack with the largest number of seats.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Track_ID, count(*) AS n FROM race GROUP BY Track_ID ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the race track that has hosted the highest number of races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM track AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T2 WHERE T2.Track_ID = T1.Track_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and location of each track that has hosted at least one race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Track_ID NOT IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race WHERE Name = 'Rolex 24 At Daytona');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all tracks that did not host the 'Rolex 24 At Daytona' race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race UNION SELECT Name FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows containing a combined, unique list of all names from both the race and track tables.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T3 JOIN track AS T2 ON T3.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all tracks that have never hosted a race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race WHERE Track_ID = (SELECT Track_ID FROM track WHERE Location = 'Fontana, CA');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the race held at the track located in 'Fontana, CA'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating = (SELECT min(T4.Seating) FROM race AS T3 JOIN track AS T4 ON T3.Track_ID = T4.Track_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of races that are held at the track(s) with the smallest seating capacity.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating < (SELECT avg(Seating) FROM track) GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any track with a below-average seating capacity that has hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID JOIN track AS T3 ON T1.Track_ID = T3.Track_ID WHERE T1.Race_ID < T2.Race_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of different races that were held at the same track, listing the names of both races and the name of the shared track.

sql: SELECT T2.Location, count(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Location HAVING count(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each track location that has hosted more than one race, along with the total number of races held at that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, CASE WHEN T2.Seating >= 80000 THEN 'Large' WHEN T2.Seating >= 60000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Small' END FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each race name, its associated track name, and a size classification ('Large', 'Medium', or 'Small') for the track based on its seating capacity.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 2000);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of races that are held at tracks opened before the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Track_ID FROM race INTERSECT SELECT Track_ID FROM track;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Year_Opened > (SELECT Year_Opened FROM track WHERE Name = 'Darlington Raceway') AND T2.Seating > (SELECT Seating FROM track WHERE Name = 'Darlington Raceway');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of races held at tracks that are both newer and have a larger seating capacity than 'Darlington Raceway'.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating, CASE WHEN Seating > 75000 THEN 'High Capacity' ELSE 'Standard Capacity' END FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each race track's name and seating capacity, along with a custom category classifying tracks with over 75,000 seats as 'High Capacity'.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.Seating) FROM track AS T1 JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT Track_ID FROM race) AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average seating capacity across all tracks that have ever hosted a race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Year_Opened FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Year_Opened > 1960;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each race and the opening year of its track, specifically for races held at tracks opened after 1960.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race WHERE Class IN (SELECT 'DP/GT');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of races that belong to the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT * FROM airport;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a complete list of all airports with their ID, city, country, IATA/ICAO codes, and full name.

sql: SELECT name, Incorporated_in FROM operate_company;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the name of each operating company and the country or region where it is incorporated.

sql: SELECT Vehicle_Flight_number, Date, Velocity FROM flight WHERE Pilot = 'Thompson';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the vehicle flight number, date, and velocity for all flights piloted by 'Thompson'.

sql: SELECT name, City FROM airport WHERE Country = 'Iceland';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and city for the airport located in Iceland.

sql: SELECT name, Principal_activities FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Corporate';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and principal activities for companies that are classified as 'Corporate'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Pilot FROM flight;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name of each unique pilot recorded in the flight data.

sql: SELECT Vehicle_Flight_number, Date FROM flight WHERE Velocity = 135;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the vehicle flight number and date for the flight that had a velocity of exactly 135.

sql: SELECT name FROM operate_company ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of all operating companies, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT IATA, ICAO, name FROM airport WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IATA and ICAO codes, and the name for each airport located in the United States.

sql: SELECT name, Group_Equity_Shareholding FROM operate_company WHERE Group_Equity_Shareholding > 50;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of companies where the group holds a majority equity shareholding (over 50%), along with the specific share percentage for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T1.Date, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the vehicle flight number and date for each flight, along with the name of the associated airport.

sql: SELECT Pilot, COUNT(*) FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pilot along with a total count of the flights they have operated.

sql: SELECT T2.City, COUNT(T1.id) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.City;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each city along with the total count of associated flights.

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.Velocity), AVG(T1.Altitude) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each operating company's name alongside the average flight velocity and average flight altitude calculated across all of its flights.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T1.Pilot FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Netherlands';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the vehicle flight number and pilot for all flights associated with airports located in the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Vehicle_Flight_number FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T1.Type = 'Joint Venture';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of companies classified as a 'Joint Venture' and their corresponding vehicle flight numbers.

sql: SELECT Pilot, COUNT(*) FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pilot who has conducted more than one flight, along with the total count of their flights.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING AVG(T1.Altitude) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of airports where the average altitude of their associated flights is greater than zero.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Incorporated_in = 'China';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the vehicle flight numbers for flights operated by companies incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of airports that have more than one flight recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Schiphol Airport';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the pilots and their operating companies for all flights associated with 'Schiphol Airport'.

sql: SELECT Pilot FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT airport_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the pilots who have flown to more than one distinct airport.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Velocity > (SELECT AVG(Velocity) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique names of airports that have been associated with flights traveling faster than the average velocity of all flights.

sql: SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Joint Venture' EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'Iceland';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of 'Joint Venture' companies that do not operate any flights associated with airports in Iceland.

sql: SELECT Vehicle_Flight_number, Pilot FROM flight WHERE company_id IN (SELECT id FROM operate_company WHERE Group_Equity_Shareholding < 50);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the vehicle flight number and pilot for flights operated by companies with a group equity shareholding of less than 50%.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS flight_count FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY flight_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single operating company with the most flights and showing its total flight count.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'United States' UNION SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of airline companies that either operate flights in the United States or are incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Country FROM airport AS T1 JOIN (SELECT airport_id FROM flight WHERE Altitude = 3650) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and country of airports associated with flights that have an altitude of exactly 3650.

sql: SELECT name FROM airport WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT airport_id FROM flight WHERE Altitude = 0);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of airports that are not associated with any flights recorded on the ground (at an altitude of 0).

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Pilot FROM flight WHERE Velocity = (SELECT MAX(Velocity) FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique pilots who have achieved the maximum recorded flight velocity.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Principal_activities = 'Cargo airline';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of cargo airlines alongside the names of the airports they service.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.airport_id) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of airline companies that operate flights associated with only a single, unique airport.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T2.Incorporated_in != T3.Country;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique names of airline companies that operate flights in countries different from where the company is incorporated.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot FROM flight AS T1 WHERE T1.company_id IN (SELECT T2.id FROM operate_company AS T2 WHERE T2.Type = 'Joint Venture');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique pilots who have flown for companies that are classified as a 'Joint Venture'.

sql: SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China' INTERSECT SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Principal_activities = 'Cargo airline';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the company that is both incorporated in China and operates as a cargo airline.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Country) AS country_count FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T1.Pilot HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Country) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pilot who has flown to more than one country, along with a count of the distinct countries they have visited.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.company_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of operating companies that do not have any associated flights.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, COUNT(T1.id) AS number_of_flights FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each country and the total number of flights associated with airports in that country.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Velocity = 240 AND T1.Altitude = 3650;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of airports and their operating airline companies for flights matching a specific velocity (240) and altitude (3650).

sql: SELECT AVG(Group_Equity_Shareholding) FROM operate_company WHERE id IN (SELECT DISTINCT company_id FROM flight);
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average Group Equity Shareholding for all companies that operate flights.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Date FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and birth date for each person in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Snatch, Clean_Jerk, Total FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the weightlifting results for each bodybuilder, detailing their 'Snatch' lift, 'Clean & Jerk' lift, and the combined 'Total' weight.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height > 190.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and height of all individuals who are taller than 190.0.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID, Snatch FROM body_builder WHERE Snatch = 140.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID for any body builder with a snatch weight of exactly 140.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Place FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Anchorage, Alaska';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person who was born in 'Anchorage, Alaska', along with their birthplace.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight FROM people ORDER BY Weight DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and weights of people, ordered from heaviest to lightest.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID, Total FROM body_builder WHERE Total > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a bodybuilder who lifted a total weight over 315.0, listing their ID and that total weight.

sql: SELECT * FROM people WHERE Name = 'Jack Campbell';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete profile information (ID, height, weight, birth date, and birthplace) for the person named 'Jack Campbell'.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Date FROM people WHERE People_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and birth date for the person with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each bodybuilder and their corresponding total score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch, T2.Clean_Jerk FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Birth_Place = 'Glenview, Illinois';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, Snatch, and Clean & Jerk stats for the bodybuilder born in 'Glenview, Illinois'.

sql: SELECT Birth_Place, AVG(Weight) FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each birthplace along with the average weight of people born in that location.

sql: SELECT Total, MAX(Snatch), MAX(Clean_Jerk) FROM body_builder GROUP BY Total;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each unique total lift weight alongside the maximum snatch and clean & jerk values achieved for that total.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > 80.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and bodybuilding totals for people who weigh more than 80.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, COUNT(T2.Body_Builder_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each distinct birth place along with the total number of bodybuilders from that location.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all individuals who are identified as bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Total) FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > 190.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average 'Total' score for bodybuilders who are taller than 190.

sql: SELECT Birth_Place FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place HAVING AVG(Height) > 180.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the birthplaces where the average height of individuals from that location exceeds 180.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 140.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person who has a snatch lift of exactly 140.0.

sql: SELECT T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Ty Conklin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the bodybuilding total for the person named 'Ty Conklin'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Total DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the top-ranked bodybuilder with the highest total score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of bodybuilders whose total score is higher than the average total score across all bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, COUNT(T2.Body_Builder_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place HAVING AVG(T1.Weight) > 85.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the total number of bodybuilders from each birthplace, for only those birthplaces where the average weight of people exceeds 85.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and snatch lift weight for bodybuilders whose height is greater than the average height across all people.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk > T2.Snatch + 30;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of bodybuilders whose 'Clean & Jerk' lift is more than 30 units heavier than their 'Snatch' lift.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan' UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight = 100.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who were either born in 'Port Huron, Michigan' or have a weight of 100.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY (T2.Snatch / T1.Weight) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the bodybuilder with the highest pound-for-pound strength, calculated as the ratio of their snatch lift to body weight.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total IN (SELECT DISTINCT Total FROM body_builder ORDER BY Total DESC LIMIT 2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and total scores for bodybuilders who achieved one of the two highest total scores.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = (SELECT MAX(T4.Clean_Jerk) FROM people AS T3 JOIN body_builder AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Birth_Place = T1.Birth_Place);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and birthplace of each bodybuilder who has the highest 'Clean & Jerk' lift compared to all other bodybuilders from the same birthplace.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > (SELECT Weight FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of people who weigh more than the individual born in 'Port Huron, Michigan'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Birth_Place = 'Anchorage, Alaska' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 137.5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any bodybuilder who was born in 'Anchorage, Alaska' and also has a snatch lift of 137.5.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total - (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder) AS Diff_From_Avg FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each body builder's name and a calculated value showing how their individual total score differs from the average total score of all body builders.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total = 315.0 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Glenview, Illinois';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a bodybuilder with a total score of 315.0, excluding any individual born in 'Glenview, Illinois'.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place ORDER BY AVG(T2.Total) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single birthplace of bodybuilders with the highest average total score.

sql: SELECT T.Birth_Place, AVG(T.Snatch) AS Avg_Snatch, AVG(T.Clean_Jerk) AS Avg_Clean_Jerk FROM (SELECT T1.Birth_Place, T2.Snatch, T2.Clean_Jerk FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID) AS T GROUP BY T.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique birthplace along with the average snatch and average clean & jerk weights for bodybuilders from that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM body_builder AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all people who are also identified as bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0 AND T1.Height < (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of bodybuilders who have a Clean & Jerk lift of 175 and are shorter than the average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place, T2.Total, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM body_builder AS T4 JOIN people AS T3 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Birth_Place = T1.Birth_Place AND T4.Total >= T2.Total) AS Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Birth_Place, Rank;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each bodybuilder's name, birthplace, and total score, along with their performance rank among others from the same birthplace, ordered by location and then by rank.

sql: SELECT Birth_Place FROM people GROUP BY Birth_Place ORDER BY AVG(Weight) ASC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the two birth places that have the lowest average weight of people, ordered from lowest to highest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight < (SELECT Weight FROM people WHERE Name = 'Al Montoya') AND T2.Total > (SELECT T4.Total FROM people AS T3 JOIN body_builder AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Al Montoya');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of any bodybuilder who weighs less than 'Al Montoya' but has a higher total lifting score.

sql: SELECT * FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, providing a complete list of all people with their corresponding ID, name, height, weight, and home town.

sql: SELECT * FROM perpetrator;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, with each row containing all available details for a specific perpetrator incident, including the date, location, and the number of people killed and injured.

sql: SELECT Name, "Home Town" FROM people WHERE People_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and home town for the person with the specific People ID of 1.

sql: SELECT * FROM perpetrator WHERE Location = 'Uiryeong';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all recorded details for the perpetrator incident that occurred in Uiryeong, including the date, country, and the number of people killed and injured.

sql: SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Location, Year FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a perpetrator from South Korea, including their ID, the location of the incident, and the year it occurred.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name, height, and weight of each person, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT Location, Country, Killed FROM perpetrator ORDER BY Killed DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing perpetrator incidents by their location and country, ordered from the highest number of people killed to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Injured FROM perpetrator ORDER BY Injured DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the perpetrator IDs who caused the most injuries, ordered from the highest number of injured to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location, T2.Country FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of perpetrators along with their associated location and country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.People_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person identified as the perpetrator with People ID 3.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfIncidents FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the total count of incidents associated with perpetrators from that country.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(Killed) AS TotalKilled, SUM(Injured) AS TotalInjured FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the total number of individuals killed and injured, grouped by year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying perpetrators who have killed more than 30 people, listing their name and the number of individuals they killed.

sql: SELECT Country FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING SUM(Killed) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing countries where the total number of people killed by perpetrators from that country is greater than 30.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Height), AVG(T1.Weight) FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average height and average weight for all individuals identified as perpetrators.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1."Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of perpetrators whose home town is 'Los Angeles, CA'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 6.5 AND People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of perpetrators who are taller than 6.5 units.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed + T2.Injured AS Total_Casualties FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY Total_Casualties DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each perpetrator's name along with the total number of casualties (killed plus injured) they caused, ordered from the highest number of casualties to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Location, Country FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'China' UNION SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Location, Country FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all perpetrators located in either China or South Korea, showing each perpetrator's ID, location, and country.

sql: SELECT T1."Home Town", COUNT(T2.Perpetrator_ID) AS PerpetratorCount FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1."Home Town";
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each hometown along with the total count of perpetrators from that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed = (SELECT MAX(Killed) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the perpetrator with the highest number of victims.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a perpetrator and their number of victims, filtered to only include perpetrators with a victim count higher than the average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Country, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed = (SELECT MAX(Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T3 WHERE T3.Country = T2.Country);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the perpetrator(s) with the highest number of killings within each country, listing their name, country, and the number of people killed.

sql: SELECT Country FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING SUM(Killed) > SUM(Injured);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the countries where the total number of people killed is greater than the total number of people injured across all incidents.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Placentia, CA';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all perpetrators who are not from Placentia, CA.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator WHERE Year = 1982.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, height, and weight of individuals who were identified as perpetrators in the year 1982.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight, T2.Injured FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > 200 AND T2.Injured > 15;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, weight, and number of injuries for perpetrators who weigh over 200 and were involved in incidents with more than 15 injuries.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T2.Killed DESC) as Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing perpetrators and the number of people they killed, along with a calculated rank from most to least people killed.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CAST(T2.Killed AS REAL) / T2.Injured AS KillToInjuryRatio FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Injured > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each perpetrator's name and their calculated kill-to-injury ratio for incidents where at least one person was injured.

sql: SELECT T1."Home Town" FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1."Home Town" ORDER BY SUM(T2.Killed) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single home town whose perpetrators are associated with the highest cumulative number of killings.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1."Home Town" = 'Baton Rouge, LA' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM perpetrator AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator) AND People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of perpetrators, excluding any who are from South Korea.

sql: SELECT Country, AVG(Killed) AS AvgKilled, AVG(Injured) AS AvgInjured FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING MAX(Killed) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for countries that have experienced at least one incident with over 30 fatalities, showing the average number of people killed and injured across all incidents for each of those countries.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Year, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Year > 1950 AND T2.Killed > 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing perpetrator names, the year of the incident, and the number of people killed for all incidents occurring after 1950 that resulted in more than 3 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > (SELECT Weight FROM people WHERE Name = 'Ron Baxter');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and locations of perpetrators who weigh more than 'Ron Baxter'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height BETWEEN 6.0 AND 6.5 AND T2.Year = 1982.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a perpetrator from the year 1982 whose height is between 6.0 and 6.5 feet.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed + T2.Injured > (SELECT AVG(Killed + Injured) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of perpetrators who caused a number of casualties (killed plus injured) greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > 200 AND T1.Height > 6.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of perpetrators who are taller than 6 feet and weigh more than 200 pounds.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who are identified as perpetrators and are also from 'Los Angeles, CA'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Country, T2.Injured, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.Country ORDER BY T2.Injured DESC) AS RankInCountry FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing perpetrators by name, country, and the number of people they injured, with each perpetrator ranked within their country from most to least injured.

sql: SELECT name, dept_name FROM student WHERE name = 'Schrefl';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each department affiliation for the student named 'Schrefl'.

sql: SELECT course_id, title FROM course WHERE credits = 3;
result_summary: Returns 108 rows listing the ID and title for every course that is worth exactly 3 credits.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT building FROM department;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each unique building that houses a department.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM takes WHERE grade = 'A-';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of course enrollments that received a grade of 'A-'.

sql: SELECT SUM(tot_cred) FROM student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total number of credits earned by all students combined.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.dept_name FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T2.building = 'Taylor';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and department of each instructor whose department is located in the 'Taylor' building.

sql: SELECT * FROM course WHERE dept_name = 'Mech. Eng.';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the full details (course ID, title, department name, and credits) for all courses offered by the Mechanical Engineering department.

sql: SELECT building, room_number FROM section WHERE semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the building and room number for each class section offered during the Fall 2010 semester.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM instructor WHERE salary < 60000;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the name and salary for each instructor earning less than $60,000.

sql: SELECT MIN(budget), MAX(budget) FROM department;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the minimum and maximum budget found across all departments.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.title FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID JOIN course AS T2 ON T3.course_id = T2.course_id;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows pairing each instructor's name with the title of a course they are assigned to teach.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, COUNT(T2.ID) FROM department AS T1 JOIN student AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name GROUP BY T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each department name along with the total number of students in that department.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T2.salary) FROM department AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name GROUP BY T1.dept_name HAVING AVG(T2.salary) > 60000;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing departments where the average instructor salary is greater than $60,000, showing each department's name and its calculated average salary.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM instructor WHERE dept_name IN (SELECT dept_name FROM department WHERE building = 'Taylor');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and salary for all instructors whose departments are located in the 'Taylor' building.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM instructor WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM instructor);
result_summary: Returns 27 rows listing the names and salaries of all instructors who earn more than the overall average salary.

sql: SELECT T2.title, COUNT(T1.ID) FROM takes AS T1 JOIN course AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.year = 2003 GROUP BY T2.title ORDER BY COUNT(T1.ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each course title and the total number of students enrolled in it during 2003, ordered from the most to least popular.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.title FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN course AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T2.grade = 'C-';
result_summary: Returns 3259 rows listing the name of each student and the title of the course for which they received a grade of 'C-'.

sql: SELECT course_id FROM course WHERE credits > 3 UNION SELECT prereq_id FROM prereq;
result_summary: Returns 136 rows containing a unique list of course IDs for all courses that are either prerequisites for another course or are worth more than 3 credits.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM instructor AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM teaches AS T2 WHERE T2.ID = T1.ID AND T2.year = 2004);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and department names of all instructors who taught a course during the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.semester, T1.year, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.course_id) FROM takes AS T1 GROUP BY T1.semester, T1.year HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.course_id) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the semester, year, and total count of distinct courses for academic terms where more than 5 unique courses were offered.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, T4.building FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.i_ID = T3.ID JOIN department AS T4 ON T3.dept_name = T4.dept_name WHERE T1.dept_name = 'History';
result_summary: Returns 117 rows listing students from the 'History' department, showing each student's name, their assigned advisor's name, and the building where the advisor's department is located.

sql: WITH DeptAvgSalary AS (SELECT dept_name, AVG(salary) as avg_sal FROM instructor GROUP BY dept_name) SELECT T1.name, T1.salary, T2.avg_sal FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN DeptAvgSalary AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T1.salary > T2.avg_sal;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing instructors who earn more than their department's average salary, showing each instructor's name, their individual salary, and the calculated average salary for their department.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T1.dept_name FROM course AS T1 WHERE T1.course_id IN (SELECT T2.course_id FROM prereq AS T2 INTERSECT SELECT T3.course_id FROM section AS T3 WHERE T3.semester = 'Fall');
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the titles and department names for courses that are both prerequisites for other courses and were offered in the 'Fall' semester.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM instructor AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.name, T2.dept_name FROM instructor AS T2 JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T2.ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.year = 2004;
result_summary: Returns 44 rows listing the names and department affiliations of all instructors who did not teach a course in the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary) FROM instructor AS T1 GROUP BY T1.dept_name HAVING AVG(T1.salary) > (SELECT AVG(T2.budget) / 20 FROM department AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing each department and its average instructor salary, filtered to only include departments where the average salary is greater than one-twentieth of the overall average departmental budget.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.tot_cred FROM student AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.ID FROM takes AS T2 GROUP BY T2.ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.course_id) > 3);
result_summary: Returns 2000 rows listing the names and total credits for all students who have taken more than three distinct courses.

sql: SELECT T1.building, T1.room_number FROM classroom AS T1 WHERE T1.capacity < (SELECT AVG(T2.capacity) FROM classroom AS T2) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM section AS T3 WHERE T3.building = T1.building AND T3.room_number = T1.room_number);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the building and room number for any classroom with a capacity below the overall average that is currently not scheduled for any sections.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM instructor AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.ID FROM teaches AS T2 JOIN section AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year WHERE T3.building = 'Chandler') AND T1.dept_name <> 'Civil Eng.';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and department of instructors who have taught in the 'Chandler' building, excluding any from the 'Civil Eng.' department.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.ID FROM student AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT T2.course_id FROM course AS T2 WHERE T2.dept_name = 'Mech. Eng.' EXCEPT SELECT T3.course_id FROM takes AS T3 WHERE T3.ID = T1.ID);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM course AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM prereq AS T2 WHERE T2.course_id = T1.course_id) AND T1.course_id IN (SELECT T3.course_id FROM takes AS T3 GROUP BY T3.course_id HAVING COUNT(T3.ID) > 5);
result_summary: Returns 37 rows listing the titles of courses that have prerequisites and an enrollment of more than 5 students.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T1.semester, COUNT(*) FROM teaches AS T1 GROUP BY T1.year, T1.semester HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM teaches WHERE year = 2004);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the academic semesters (year and semester) that had a higher number of teaching assignments than the total number of assignments for the entire year 2004, along with the specific count for each of those busier semesters.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM student AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.ID FROM takes AS T2 WHERE T2.course_id = '313' AND T2.year = 2010) AND T1.ID IN (SELECT T3.ID FROM takes AS T3 WHERE T3.course_id = '401' AND T3.year = 2003);
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the names and department names of students who took both course '313' in 2010 and course '401' in 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name FROM student AS T1 WHERE T1.tot_cred > 100 GROUP BY T1.dept_name EXCEPT SELECT T2.dept_name FROM student AS T2 WHERE T2.tot_cred < 50 GROUP BY T2.dept_name;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.course_id, T2.prereq_id FROM prereq AS T1 JOIN prereq AS T2 ON T1.prereq_id = T2.course_id;
result_summary: Returns 56 rows, each identifying a two-step prerequisite relationship by listing a course ID and the prerequisite of its own prerequisite.

sql: SELECT T1.name, CASE WHEN T1.salary > 100000 THEN 'High Earner' WHEN T1.salary > 60000 THEN 'Mid Earner' ELSE 'Standard Earner' END AS category FROM instructor AS T1 WHERE T1.dept_name IN (SELECT dept_name FROM department WHERE budget > 500000);
result_summary: Returns 29 rows listing instructors from high-budget departments (budget > $500,000) and classifying their salary into categories such as 'High Earner', 'Mid Earner', or 'Standard Earner'.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name FROM instructor AS T1 GROUP BY T1.dept_name ORDER BY AVG(T1.salary) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the highest average instructor salary.

sql: SELECT T1.day, T1.start_hr, T1.start_min, COUNT(*) FROM time_slot AS T1 JOIN section AS T2 ON T1.time_slot_id = T2.time_slot_id GROUP BY T1.day, T1.start_hr, T1.start_min ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows that count the number of course sections scheduled at each unique time slot, ordered from the most frequently used time slots to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.course_id, T1.year FROM teaches AS T1 GROUP BY T1.course_id, T1.year HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the specific course and year that was taught by more than one instructor.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.title FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID JOIN course AS T2 ON T3.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE (T3.course_id, T3.sec_id, T3.semester, T3.year) IN (SELECT course_id, sec_id, semester, year FROM section WHERE building = 'Chandler' AND year = 2010);
result_summary: Returns 270 rows listing the name of each student and the title of the course they took for all classes held in the 'Chandler' building during 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.i_ID GROUP BY T1.ID, T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.s_ID) > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(s_ID) AS c FROM advisor GROUP BY i_ID));
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the names of instructors who advise more students than the overall average number of advisees per instructor.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_Deaths FROM storm ORDER BY Number_Deaths DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing storm names and the number of deaths they caused, ordered from the deadliest storm to the least.

sql: SELECT Region_code, Region_name FROM region ORDER BY Region_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each region's code and name, ordered alphabetically by the region's name.

sql: SELECT Name, Damage_millions_USD FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > 10.75;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that caused more than 10.75 million USD in damage, along with the specific damage amount.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths = 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that resulted in no deaths.

sql: SELECT Region_name FROM region WHERE Region_code = 'AF';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the region that corresponds to the region code 'AF'.

sql: SELECT Name, Damage_millions_USD FROM storm ORDER BY Damage_millions_USD DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three most financially damaging storms, ordered from highest to lowest cost in millions of USD.

sql: SELECT Name, Max_speed FROM storm WHERE Max_speed = 972;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that have a maximum speed of exactly 972.

sql: SELECT * FROM storm;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all recorded storms and their complete details, including active dates, maximum speed, financial damage, and number of deaths.

sql: SELECT * FROM storm WHERE Name = 'One';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing all recorded details, such as dates active, max speed, and damages, for the storm specifically named 'One'.

sql: SELECT Storm_ID, Region_id FROM affected_region WHERE Number_city_affected = 10.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the Storm ID and Region ID for any storm event that affected exactly 10 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing storm names and the corresponding regions they affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Region_id) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name, along with a count of the number of regions it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, COUNT(T2.Storm_ID) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T1.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region name along with the total count of storms that have affected that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name and the corresponding total number of cities it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T3.Region_name = 'Afghanistan';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that have affected the region of Afghanistan.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Name = 'Three';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the region that was affected by the storm named 'Three'.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, AVG(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T1.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region name along with the average number of cities affected within that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Number_Deaths > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that caused fatalities and the corresponding regions they affected.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Max_speed > 990;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique regions that have been affected by storms with a maximum speed greater than 990.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, SUM(T1.Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id GROUP BY T3.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each geographical region and the total financial damage (in millions of USD) from all storms that occurred there.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT AVG(Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that caused financial damage greater than the average damage of all storms.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_Deaths FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths = (SELECT MAX(Number_Deaths) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the storm with the highest number of deaths, showing its name and the total death count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Region_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that have affected more than one region.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name FROM region AS T3 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T3.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T1 ON T2.Storm_ID = T1.Storm_ID WHERE T1.Number_Deaths = 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of regions that have been affected by storms with zero recorded deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 1) AND T1.Storm_ID NOT IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region WHERE Region_id = 3);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that affected Region 1 but did not affect Region 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T1.Region_name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Storm_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the region most frequently affected by storms, determined by counting the number of storms for each region.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, MAX(T1.Max_speed) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id GROUP BY T3.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each geographical region along with the maximum speed of any storm that has affected it.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT Damage_millions_USD FROM storm WHERE Name = 'Two');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that caused more financial damage than the storm named 'Two'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID HAVING SUM(Number_city_affected) > 20);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that affected a total of more than 20 cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM affected_region AS T2 WHERE T2.Region_id = T1.Region_id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM affected_region AS T2 JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = T1.Region_id AND T3.Damage_millions_USD > 13.0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of regions that have been affected by storms, but have never experienced a storm causing more than $13 million in damages.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Max_speed = 972 UNION SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 50;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of storms that either had a maximum speed of 972 or resulted in more than 50 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = (SELECT Region_id FROM region WHERE Region_name = 'Afghanistan');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that have affected the region of 'Afghanistan'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the storm that has affected both region ID 1 and region ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a storm that affected region 1 and resulted in zero deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_name HAVING SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each region that has experienced storm-related fatalities, along with the total count of deaths for that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.total_cities FROM storm AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Storm_ID, SUM(Number_city_affected) AS total_cities FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.total_cities > 20;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of major storms that affected more than 20 cities, along with the total count of cities affected by each.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_code FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_code HAVING SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the region codes for regions where the total accumulated storm damage exceeds $10 million.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Max_speed < (SELECT AVG(Max_speed) FROM storm) AND Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that had a below-average maximum speed and are known to have impacted a region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name, T2.Number_city_affected FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T2.Number_city_affected > (SELECT AVG(Number_city_affected) FROM affected_region);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying storms that impacted a higher-than-average number of cities, listing the storm's name, the affected region, and the corresponding count of cities affected.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD = 13.0);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of regions affected by storms that caused exactly 13.0 million USD in damages.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE lname = 'Gibbons';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of any author whose last name is 'Gibbons'.

sql: SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of institutions that are located in the USA.

sql: SELECT title FROM Papers ORDER BY title ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the titles of all available papers, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Authors;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value: the total count of all authors.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID = 51;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first and last name for the specific author with an ID of 51.

sql: SELECT title FROM Papers WHERE paperID = 200;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the title of the paper corresponding to paper ID 200.

sql: SELECT T1.lname, T2.paperID FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows, pairing an author's last name with the ID of each paper they have authored to show all authorship instances.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.instID FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows, linking each paper's title to the institution ID of one of its authors, with a row generated for each author-paper combination.

sql: SELECT lname FROM Authors ORDER BY lname;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the last names of all authors, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT name, country FROM Inst ORDER BY name LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the names and countries of the first two institutions, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT T1.lname, COUNT(T2.paperID) FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each author's last name along with the total count of papers they have written.

sql: SELECT T3.country, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.authID) FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID GROUP BY T3.country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the total count of unique authors affiliated with an institution in that country.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.name = 'University of Oxford';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of authors who are affiliated with the 'University of Oxford'.

sql: SELECT T3.title FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T2.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.lname = 'Hinze';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of academic papers written by an author with the last name 'Hinze'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID HAVING count(*) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of institutions that have contributed to two or more publications.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of authors who are designated as the primary author on a publication.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.title FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique titles of papers that have at least one author from an institution located in the UK.

sql: SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING count(paperID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID of an author who has written more than one paper.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T2.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T3.title LIKE '%Simple Monadic%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the unique name of the institution that has authored a paper with 'Simple Monadic' in its title.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE instID = 1000);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of authors affiliated with the institution identified by ID 1000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.paperID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the names of institutions that have authored more than one distinct paper.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID ORDER BY count(T2.paperID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the institution that has authored the most papers.

sql: SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T2.authID = T3.authID WHERE T1.authID = 50 AND T2.authID != 50;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of an author who has co-authored a paper with the author identified by ID 50.
