sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK' EXCEPT SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country != 'UK';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of authors whose institutional affiliations are exclusively within the UK.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID GROUP BY T1.paperID ORDER BY count(T2.authID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the title of the paper with the highest number of co-authors.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname, COUNT(T2.paperID) FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.name = 'University of Oxford' GROUP BY T1.authID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last name of each author from the 'University of Oxford' along with the total number of papers they have authored.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID NOT IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of authors who have never been the primary author (first in order) on any publication.

sql: SELECT T3.lname, T4.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID AND T1.authID < T2.authID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T1.authID = T3.authID JOIN Authors AS T4 ON T2.authID = T4.authID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing unique pairs of author last names who have co-authored at least one paper together.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.authID) >= COUNT(DISTINCT T1.paperID);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of institutions where the number of unique authors is greater than or equal to the number of unique papers they have published.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.name = 'University of Oxford' GROUP BY T1.paperID HAVING COUNT(T2.authID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of academic papers from the 'University of Oxford' that have at least two authors from that same institution.

sql: SELECT T1.authID FROM Authorship AS T1 WHERE T1.authOrder = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.authID FROM Authorship AS T2 WHERE T2.authOrder = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of an author who has served as both a first author and a second author on publications.

sql: SELECT T3.name, AVG(T3.author_count) FROM (SELECT T2.name, T1.paperID, COUNT(T1.authID) AS author_count FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T2.name, T1.paperID) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each institution's name and the calculated average number of authors per paper associated with that institution.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT authID FROM Authorship);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.country FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID GROUP BY T3.country ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.authID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country with the highest number of distinct authors.

sql: SELECT T1.paperID, count(T1.authID) FROM Authorship AS T1 GROUP BY T1.paperID HAVING count(T1.authID) > (SELECT avg(T2.c) FROM (SELECT count(authID) AS c FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID) AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a paper with a higher-than-average number of authors, showing the paper's ID and its specific author count.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.instID = 1000 UNION SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE lname = 'James';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of authors who are either affiliated with institution 1000 or have the last name 'James'.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname, T3.title FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T1.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.paperID IN (SELECT paperID FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID HAVING count(authID) = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, last name, and title for a paper that was written by a single author.

sql: SELECT T1.paperID FROM Authorship AS T1 WHERE T1.authID = 51 EXCEPT SELECT T2.paperID FROM Authorship AS T2 WHERE T2.authID = 50;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the ID of a paper authored by author 51 but not by author 50.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.authOrder = (SELECT MAX(authOrder) FROM Authorship);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the first and last name of the author with the highest authorship order number, representing the last author on the publication with the most co-authors.

sql: SELECT T1.authID FROM (SELECT authID, COUNT(paperID) AS paper_count FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID) AS T1 WHERE T1.paper_count > (SELECT AVG(T2.paper_count) FROM (SELECT authID, COUNT(paperID) AS paper_count FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID) AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID of an author who has published a number of papers greater than the average number of papers per author.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Decoration_Theme FROM round;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique decoration themes used across all rounds.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each member's name and the name of their corresponding college.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Member_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member identified by Member_ID 1.

sql: SELECT Rank_in_Round FROM round WHERE Member_ID = 2 AND Round_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the rank achieved by the specific member with ID 2 in round 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Member_ID) FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each college's name along with the total number of members in that college.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Decoration_Theme FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows pairing each member's name with the decoration theme of the rounds they participated in.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name, T2.College_Location FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names and locations of colleges that have members from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Rank_in_Round) FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each member's name along with their average rank calculated across all rounds played.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Member_ID NOT IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member WHERE Country = 'Canada');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all members who are not from Canada.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE College_ID = (SELECT College_ID FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all members who are associated with the 'Saskatchewan School' college.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Decoration_Theme FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T3.Round_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying each member who participated in the first round of an event, showing their name, their college's name, and their chosen decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of colleges that have more than one member.

sql: SELECT MIN(T2.Rank_in_Round) FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the single best (lowest) rank achieved by any member from the 'United States'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Country FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the unique country of members who have ranked first in any round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM round AS T2 WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T2.Rank_in_Round = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who have finished first in at least one round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.College_ID = (SELECT T2.College_ID FROM member AS T2 WHERE T2.Name = 'Jack Nicklaus') AND T1.Name <> 'Jack Nicklaus';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the member who attended the same college as 'Jack Nicklaus'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'United States' GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of colleges that have more than one member from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Decoration_Theme = 'Walk on the Moon') AND T1.Member_ID NOT IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Decoration_Theme = 'Soft Dream');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member who participated in a 'Walk on the Moon' themed round but not in a 'Soft Dream' themed round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Member_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the college with the highest number of members.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Round_ID = 1 AND T2.Rank_in_Round < (SELECT AVG(Rank_in_Round) FROM round WHERE Round_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from Round 1 who achieved a rank that was better than the average rank for that round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'United States' UNION SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Country = 'Canada';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all members from Canada and any members from the United States who have participated in a round.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T1.College_Location = 'Ottawa' AND T3.Round_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from colleges in 'Ottawa' who participated in the first round of the competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM round AS T2 WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T2.Decoration_Theme LIKE '%Dark%') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM round AS T3 WHERE T3.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T3.Decoration_Theme NOT LIKE '%Dark%');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the member who has exclusively participated in rounds with a 'Dark' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T1.Member_ID < T2.Member_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, with each row containing the names of a unique pair of members who attended the same college.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Round_ID) FROM member AS T1 LEFT JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each member's name and the total count of rounds they have played, including members who have not played any rounds.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Decoration_Theme = 'Soft Dream' AND T2.Rank_in_Round = (SELECT MAX(Rank_in_Round) FROM round WHERE Decoration_Theme = 'Soft Dream');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member who achieved the highest rank in any round with the 'Soft Dream' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Member_ID NOT IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of members who have participated in a round but have never won (i.e., never achieved a rank of 1).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, MIN(T3.Rank_in_Round) FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each college's name alongside the best (minimum) rank achieved by any of its members in a round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Round_ID = 1 AND T2.Rank_in_Round = (SELECT T4.Rank_in_Round FROM member AS T3 JOIN round AS T4 ON T3.Member_ID = T4.Member_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Billy Casper' AND T4.Round_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the member who achieved the same rank as 'Billy Casper' in Round 1.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE College_ID NOT IN (SELECT College_ID FROM college);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM (SELECT Name, Country, 1 AS sort_order FROM member WHERE Country = 'United States' UNION ALL SELECT Name, Country, 2 AS sort_order FROM member WHERE Country = 'Canada') AS T1 ORDER BY T1.sort_order;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and country for members from the United States and Canada, ordered to show all US members first.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Member_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Round_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Round_ID) FROM round);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE College_ID = (SELECT College_ID FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School') UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Decoration_Theme = 'Dark Nights';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of members who either attended the 'Saskatchewan School' or participated in a round with the 'Dark Nights' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Round_ID = 1 AND T2.Rank_in_Round > (SELECT Rank_in_Round FROM round WHERE Decoration_Theme = 'Walk on the Moon');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from Round 1 who had a rank numerically greater than the rank associated with the 'Walk on the Moon' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT Title, Original_air_date FROM film WHERE Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the title and original air date for all films directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM cinema ORDER BY Capacity DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing all cinemas and their seating capacity, ordered from highest to lowest capacity.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the total count of all films.

sql: SELECT AVG(Capacity) FROM cinema;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average capacity across all cinemas.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Date FROM schedule;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all the unique dates found in the schedule.

sql: SELECT Name, Openning_year FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year > 2010;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and opening year for all cinemas that opened after 2010.

sql: SELECT Cinema_ID, Film_ID, Date FROM schedule WHERE Price = 12.99;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the cinema ID, film ID, and date for all movie showings with a ticket price of $12.99.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Directed_by FROM film;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of every film director in the database.

sql: SELECT T2.Date FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Codling';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the scheduled dates for all showings at the cinema named 'Codling'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of all films scheduled to be shown on '21 May'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each cinema by name and the total count of films scheduled for each location.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, AVG(T2.Price) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each film title along with its average ticket price.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Cinema_ID = (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM cinema WHERE Name = 'Codling'));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of all films scheduled to play at the cinema named 'Codling'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the cinema that is showing more than one film.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Name, T2.Location FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T3 ON T1.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T2 ON T3.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the title of each film showing on May 21st, along with the name and location of the cinema where it is playing.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Price = 8.99);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of any film scheduled to be shown at a price of $8.99.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM cinema WHERE Capacity > (SELECT AVG(Capacity) FROM cinema);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and capacities of cinemas that have a seating capacity greater than the average across all cinemas.

sql: SELECT Date, SUM(Show_times_per_day) AS TotalShowings FROM schedule GROUP BY Date ORDER BY TotalShowings DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the date that had the highest total number of movie showings, along with that total count.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'County Wicklow';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of all films playing in cinemas located in County Wicklow.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner' AND T2.Show_times_per_day > 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the titles of films directed by 'Bill Schreiner' that are scheduled to be shown more than twice per day.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 WHERE T1.Capacity > (SELECT AVG(T2.Capacity) FROM cinema AS T2) AND T1.Cinema_ID IN (SELECT T3.Cinema_ID FROM schedule AS T3 GROUP BY T3.Cinema_ID HAVING COUNT(T3.Film_ID) >= 3);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a cinema that has an above-average capacity and has shown at least three different films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Name FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the film title and the name of the cinema where the ticket price is the highest of all available showtimes.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM cinema AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM schedule AS T2 WHERE T2.Cinema_ID = T1.Cinema_ID AND T2.Date = '21 Jun');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and location for all cinemas that have no scheduled events or showings on June 21st.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Show_times_per_day > 3 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films that are both frequently shown (more than 3 times per day) and have a ticket price greater than 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 WHERE T1.Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Cinema_ID = 1) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 WHERE T1.Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Date = '21 Jun');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films scheduled at Cinema #1, excluding any films that are also scheduled for June 21st.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.avg_price FROM (SELECT Date, AVG(Price) AS avg_price FROM schedule GROUP BY Date) AS T1 WHERE T1.avg_price > 10;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the dates and their corresponding average schedule price, filtered to only include days where that average price is greater than 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Openning_year, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM schedule AS T2 WHERE T2.Cinema_ID = T1.Cinema_ID) AS NumberOfScheduledFilms FROM cinema AS T1 ORDER BY NumberOfScheduledFilms DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each cinema's name, opening year, and the total count of films scheduled, ordered from the cinema with the most scheduled films to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title HAVING SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) > (SELECT AVG(Show_times_per_day) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films whose total number of daily showings is greater than the average number of showings across all scheduled films.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 WHERE T1.Cinema_ID IN (SELECT T2.Cinema_ID FROM schedule AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Cinema_ID HAVING MIN(T2.Price) < 9);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any cinema that has at least one movie showing with a ticket price of less than 9.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Name FROM film AS T1, cinema AS T2 WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner' AND T2.Location = 'County Wicklow' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM schedule AS T3 WHERE T3.Film_ID = T1.Film_ID AND T3.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of films directed by 'Bill Schreiner' and the names of cinemas in 'County Wicklow' where those films are scheduled to be shown.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM cinema WHERE Cinema_ID NOT IN (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and location of cinemas that currently have no scheduled movies.

sql: WITH CinemaFilmCounts AS (SELECT Cinema_ID, COUNT(Film_ID) as film_count FROM schedule GROUP BY Cinema_ID) SELECT T1.Name, T2.film_count FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN CinemaFilmCounts AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T1.Capacity > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of each cinema with a capacity over 1000, along with the total count of films scheduled at that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Original_air_date FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Film_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Cinema_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the title and original air date of the film that has been scheduled to play in more than one cinema.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by, SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS TotalShowings FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by ORDER BY TotalShowings DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the director with the highest total number of daily showings across all of their films, along with that total sum.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' UNION SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price = 8.99;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique titles of films that are either scheduled to be shown on '21 May' or have a ticket price of 8.99.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Name FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Show_times_per_day = (SELECT MAX(T4.Show_times_per_day) FROM schedule AS T4 WHERE T4.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the films that have the maximum number of daily showings for each cinema, providing the film title and the corresponding cinema name.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, COUNT(T1.Film_ID) FROM schedule AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Date HAVING AVG(T1.Price) < (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing dates where the average ticket price was below the overall average, showing the total number of films scheduled for each of those dates.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T3.TotalShowTimes FROM (SELECT Cinema_ID, SUM(Show_times_per_day) as TotalShowTimes FROM schedule GROUP BY Cinema_ID) AS T3 JOIN cinema AS T2 ON T3.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.TotalShowTimes > 5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and total daily showtimes for any cinema that has more than 5 shows per day.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 WHERE T1.Rank_in_series > 26 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Title FROM film AS T2 JOIN schedule AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T3.Price < 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of late-series films (ranked above 26) that do not have a scheduled showing with a ticket price under $10.

sql: SELECT Name, Openning_year FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year = (SELECT MIN(Openning_year) FROM cinema) AND Cinema_ID IN (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM schedule);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color, interface FROM product WHERE dpi = 600.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the product name, color pages-per-minute speed, and interface for all products with a 600 DPI resolution.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Area_size FROM store WHERE Type = 'Village Store';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and area size of each store classified as a 'Village Store'.

sql: SELECT District_name, City_Population FROM district ORDER BY City_Population DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each district's name and its city's population, ordered from the most to least populous city.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Type FROM store WHERE Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-ranked store, showing its name and type.

sql: SELECT * FROM product WHERE product = 'Xerox Travel Scanner 100';
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing all available specifications, such as dimensions, DPI, and scanning speed, for the 'Xerox Travel Scanner 100' product.

sql: SELECT District_name FROM district WHERE City_Area = 20.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the district that has a city area of exactly 20.0.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store ORDER BY Store_Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each store, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Type FROM store;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique types of stores available.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE interface = 'USB 2.0';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and color pages-per-minute speed for all products that connect via a 'USB 2.0' interface.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Area_size FROM store WHERE Area_size > 100;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and area size for each store with an area size larger than 100.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, COUNT(T2.Product_ID) FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each store's name along with a count of the total number of products carried by that store.

sql: SELECT T1.product, COUNT(T2.Store_ID) FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID GROUP BY T1.product;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product name along with the total number of stores that carry it.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each store's name and the name of the district it belongs to.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.City_Population) FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average city population for districts that contain a store.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Number_of_product_category FROM store WHERE Number_of_product_category > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing stores that offer more than 1000 product categories, showing each store's name and its specific number of categories.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.Product_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of stores that carry the product with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID WHERE T1.Store_Name = 'Miramichi';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the district where the store named 'Miramichi' is located.

sql: SELECT T2.District_name, COUNT(T1.Store_ID) FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T2.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each district by name along with the total count of stores within that district.

sql: SELECT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Type = 'City Mall';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the products available at stores classified as the 'City Mall' type.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Ranking FROM store WHERE Ranking > (SELECT AVG(Ranking) FROM store);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and rankings of stores that have a ranking greater than the overall average ranking.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product = 'Fujitsu fi-6130 A4 Series Scanner';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all stores that sell the 'Fujitsu fi-6130 A4 Series Scanner' product.

sql: SELECT T2.District_name FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T2.District_name HAVING count(T1.Store_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the names of districts that contain more than one store.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Area_size, Ranking FROM store WHERE Ranking = (SELECT MIN(Ranking) FROM store);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the store with the best (lowest) ranking, including its name, area size, and rank.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID WHERE T2.Store_ID IN (SELECT T3.Store_ID FROM store_district AS T3 JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID WHERE T4.City_Population > 100000);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique products sold in stores located in cities with a population greater than 100,000.

sql: SELECT T3.District_name, AVG(T1.Area_size) FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID GROUP BY T3.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district name along with the average area size of the stores within that district.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID WHERE T1.Type = 'Village Store' AND T3.City_Area < 20.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of 'Village Stores' that are located in districts with a city area of less than 20.

sql: SELECT product FROM product WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT Product_ID FROM store_product WHERE Store_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a product that is not available at the store with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T2.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T1.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN district AS T2 ON T3.District_ID = T2.District_ID WHERE T1.Type IN ('City Mall', 'Village Store') GROUP BY T2.District_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Type) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the district that contains both a 'City Mall' and a 'Village Store'.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.Product_ID = 1 ORDER BY T1.Area_size DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the largest store by area size that carries the specific product with ID 1.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store UNION SELECT District_name FROM district;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the unique names of all stores and districts, consolidated into a single list.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.Product_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of stores that are located in District 15 and also sell Product 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.District_ID = 15 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID WHERE T2.Product_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of a store within District 15 that does not carry Product 2.

sql: SELECT T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID WHERE T1.Area_size = (SELECT MAX(Area_size) FROM store);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the district that contains the store with the largest area size.

sql: SELECT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the products available for sale in the store with the highest ranking.

sql: SELECT T1.product, AVG(T3.Area_size) FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.product;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product along with the average area size of the stores where it is sold.

sql: SELECT T4.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T1.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID GROUP BY T4.District_name ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Product_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single district that offers the highest number of unique products across its stores.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T2.Store_Name, T5.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T1.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID JOIN store AS T2 ON T2.Store_ID = T4.Store_ID JOIN district AS T5 ON T3.District_ID = T5.District_ID WHERE T1.Store_ID < T2.Store_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of different stores that are located in the same district, listing the names of both stores and their shared district name.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.dpi = 600.0 GROUP BY T1.Store_Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.product_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM product WHERE dpi = 600.0);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T4.District_name, COUNT(T2.Product_ID) AS Total_Products FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T1.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID GROUP BY T4.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each district name alongside the total count of products offered by all stores within that district.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product, T4.District_name FROM product AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.Product_ID JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the unique combinations of products and the districts where they are sold.

sql: SELECT * FROM phone;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all phones, showing their name, ID, memory size, carrier, and price.

sql: SELECT * FROM market;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, providing a complete list of every market along with its district, number of employees, number of shops, and ranking.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone ORDER BY Price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each phone's name and price, ordered from the highest price to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Name, Memory_in_G FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and memory in Gigabytes for phones available from the 'Sprint' carrier.

sql: SELECT * FROM market WHERE District = 'Alberta';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with complete details, including Market ID, number of employees, number of shops, and ranking, for the market located in the 'Alberta' district.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Price > 320.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and price for all phones priced above 320.0.

sql: SELECT District, Num_of_shops FROM market WHERE Num_of_employees = 1966;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the district and the number of shops for any market that has exactly 1966 employees.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Carrier FROM phone;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique mobile carrier available in the dataset.

sql: SELECT * FROM phone WHERE Name = 'IPhone 6s';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details, including the Phone ID, memory, carrier, and price, for the phone model 'IPhone 6s'.

sql: SELECT District, Num_of_shops FROM market WHERE Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the district and number of shops for the market that has the top ranking.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Num_of_stock FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'IPhone 5s';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the stock count for the 'IPhone 5s' model in each market where it is available.

sql: SELECT T1.District, COUNT(T2.Phone_ID) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district and the corresponding total count of phones within that district.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each phone model name and its corresponding total number of units in stock across all markets.

sql: SELECT Carrier, AVG(Price) FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each mobile carrier along with the average price for phones on that carrier.

sql: SELECT T1.District, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) AS TotalStock FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying districts with a total phone stock exceeding 1000 units, showing the name of each district and its corresponding total stock count.

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM phone);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the most expensive phone in the dataset.

sql: SELECT District FROM market WHERE Num_of_shops > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_shops) FROM market);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the districts that have an above-average number of shops compared to all markets.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Market_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and price of each phone available in the specific market identified by Market_ID 1.

sql: SELECT Carrier, count(*) FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each mobile carrier and the total count of phones for that carrier.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, T2.District FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T2 ON T3.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each phone's name, its price, and the district of the market where it is sold.

sql: SELECT T1.District, T2.Name, T3.Num_of_stock FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T2 ON T3.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.District = 'Alberta';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and stock count for each phone available in markets within the 'Alberta' district.

sql: SELECT T2.Carrier, SUM(T3.Num_of_stock) FROM phone AS T2 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T1 ON T3.Market_ID = T1.Market_ID GROUP BY T2.Carrier;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows detailing the total number of phones in stock for each carrier across all markets.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Phone_ID IN (SELECT Phone_ID FROM phone_market WHERE Market_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the names and prices for phones sold in the market identified by Market ID 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM phone);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and price for all phones with a price above the average.

sql: SELECT T1.District FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Phone_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the districts where more than one distinct phone model is available across its markets.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM phone_market AS T1 JOIN phone AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Market_ID = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM phone_market AS T1 JOIN phone AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Market_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of phones sold in market 1 that are not also sold in market 2.

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Memory_in_G = 128;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a phone model that is available on the 'Sprint' carrier and has 128GB of memory.

sql: SELECT T.District FROM (SELECT T1.District, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) AS TotalStock FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District) AS T WHERE T.TotalStock > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the districts where the total phone stock, summed across all markets within that district, is greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint' UNION SELECT District FROM market WHERE Ranking < 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the combined names of phones from the 'Sprint' carrier and districts from top-ranked markets (those with a ranking less than 5).

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Phone_ID NOT IN (SELECT Phone_ID FROM phone_market WHERE Market_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of phones that are not available in the market with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.District, AVG(T2.Price) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T2 ON T3.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING AVG(T2.Price) > 300;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing districts where the average phone price is greater than 300, showing each district and its calculated average price.

sql: SELECT District, Num_of_employees FROM market AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM phone_market AS T2 WHERE T2.Market_ID = T1.Market_ID AND T2.Num_of_stock > 4000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the district and number of employees for markets that have more than 4000 phones in stock.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.District FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T2 ON T3.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Memory_in_G = 128 AND T1.Ranking = 21;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a phone with 128GB of memory and its corresponding market district, filtered specifically for markets that have a ranking of 21.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Price > (SELECT Price FROM phone WHERE Name = 'IPhone 5s');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and price for all phones that are more expensive than the 'IPhone 5s'.

sql: SELECT Name, Carrier, Price FROM phone AS T1 WHERE Price = (SELECT MAX(T2.Price) FROM phone AS T2 WHERE T2.Carrier = T1.Carrier);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the most expensive phone for each distinct carrier, showing the phone's name and price.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM phone AS T1 WHERE T1.Carrier = 'Sprint' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM phone AS T2 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T4 ON T3.Market_ID = T4.Market_ID WHERE T4.District = 'Alberta';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a phone that is both carried by 'Sprint' and sold in the 'Alberta' district.

sql: SELECT T1.District FROM market AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.District FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T3.Carrier = 'Sprint';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the districts where no phones from the 'Sprint' carrier are sold.

sql: SELECT Name, Price, Carrier FROM phone AS T1 WHERE T1.Price > (SELECT AVG(T2.Price) FROM phone AS T2 WHERE T2.Carrier = T1.Carrier);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, price, and carrier for phones that are priced higher than the average for their respective carriers.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price FROM phone AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Carrier FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier HAVING COUNT(*) = 1) AS T2 ON T1.Carrier = T2.Carrier;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.District, SUM(T3.Num_of_stock), COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Phone_ID), AVG(T1.Ranking) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, with each row representing a district and showing its total phone stock, the number of unique phone models available, and the average market ranking for that district.

sql: SELECT company_name, company_address FROM Third_Party_Companies WHERE company_type = 'Maintenance Contractor';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and address for each company classified as a 'Maintenance Contractor'.

sql: SELECT part_name, chargeable_amount FROM Parts WHERE chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and chargeable amount for all parts that are designated as chargeable.

sql: SELECT skill_description FROM Skills WHERE skill_code = 'ELEC';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the full skill description for the skill with the code 'ELEC'.

sql: SELECT staff_id, staff_name FROM Staff ORDER BY staff_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the ID and name for every staff member, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Assets;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of all assets.

sql: SELECT fault_log_entry_id, recorded_by_staff_id, fault_log_entry_datetime FROM Fault_Log WHERE fault_description = 'system error';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all fault log entries classified as a 'system error', showing the entry ID, the ID of the staff member who recorded it, and the date and time of the entry.

sql: SELECT asset_details, asset_model, asset_acquired_date FROM Assets WHERE asset_make = 'PT';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details, model, and acquired date for all assets manufactured by 'PT'.

sql: SELECT part_fault_id, fault_description FROM Part_Faults WHERE fault_short_name = 'PW';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the specific fault ID and description for all part faults recorded with the short name 'PW'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.part_id FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id WHERE T1.asset_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the details and constituent part IDs for the specific asset with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.company_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each maintenance engineer by their first and last name, along with the name of the third-party company they are employed by.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, count(T2.skill_id) FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id GROUP BY T1.engineer_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each maintenance engineer by first and last name, along with a count of the total number of skills they possess.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, count(T2.part_id) AS NumberOfParts FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id GROUP BY T1.asset_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing the details for each asset along with a total count of its associated parts.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id GROUP BY T1.engineer_id HAVING count(T2.skill_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers who possess more than one skill.

sql: SELECT asset_id, asset_details FROM Assets WHERE asset_id IN (SELECT asset_id FROM Fault_Log);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the ID and details for all assets that have a recorded fault in the fault log.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.skill_description FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows that pair each maintenance engineer's first and last name with the description of a specific skill they possess.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.part_name FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id WHERE T2.fault_description = 'Top Parts Wrong';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of parts that have been recorded with the specific fault description 'Top Parts Wrong'.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id WHERE T2.asset_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of staff members who have recorded a fault for the asset with ID 3.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id WHERE T2.fault_status = 'Waiting';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique first and last names of maintenance engineers who are assigned to a fault visit with a 'Waiting' status.

sql: SELECT chargeable_yn, AVG(chargeable_amount) FROM Parts GROUP BY chargeable_yn;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the average chargeable amount for parts, grouped by whether they are chargeable or not.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.company_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.supplier_company_id = T2.company_id WHERE strftime('%Y', T1.asset_acquired_date) = '2018';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details of an asset acquired in 2018 and the name of its corresponding supplier company.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T4 ON T1.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Electrical' AND T4.fault_status = 'Waiting';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers with an 'Electrical' skill who are assigned to a job with a 'Waiting' fault status.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, count(T2.engineer_id) AS number_of_engineers FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.company_id ORDER BY number_of_engineers DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the third-party company that employs the most maintenance engineers, along with the total count of those engineers.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.part_name FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T3 ON T2.part_fault_id = T3.part_fault_id JOIN Skills AS T4 ON T3.skill_id = T4.skill_id WHERE T4.skill_description = 'Mechanical';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of parts that require a 'Mechanical' skill for fault repair.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id JOIN Assets AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T4 ON T3.supplier_company_id = T4.company_id WHERE T4.company_name = 'Langworth-Funk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a staff member who has logged a fault for an asset supplied by the company 'Langworth-Funk'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Electrical';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of all maintenance engineers who do not possess the 'Electrical' skill.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_make, count(T2.fault_log_entry_id) FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id GROUP BY T1.asset_make HAVING count(T2.fault_log_entry_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each asset make that has more than one recorded fault, along with the total count of faults for that make.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Part_Faults AS T3 ON T2.part_id = T3.part_id JOIN Maintenance_Contracts AS T4 ON T1.maintenance_contract_id = T4.maintenance_contract_id WHERE T3.fault_short_name = 'TW' AND T4.maintenance_contract_company_id = 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the ID and details for an asset maintained by company 15 that has experienced a part fault with the short name 'TW'.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, T2.FaultCount FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T3.company_id, COUNT(T5.fault_log_entry_id) AS FaultCount FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T3 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T5 ON T4.fault_log_entry_id = T5.fault_log_entry_id GROUP BY T3.company_id) AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.FaultCount > 0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each third-party company that has addressed at least one fault, along with the total count of faults handled by their engineers.

sql: SELECT maintenance_contract_id, contract_start_date, contract_end_date FROM Maintenance_Contracts ORDER BY julianday(contract_end_date) - julianday(contract_start_date) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the longest-running maintenance contract by duration, listing its ID, start date, and end date.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'McDermott Group' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'TV, Video';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of maintenance engineers who either work for the 'McDermott Group' or are skilled in 'TV, Video'.

sql: SELECT T1.part_name, count(*) AS fault_count FROM Parts AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T2 ON T1.part_id = (SELECT part_id FROM Part_Faults WHERE part_fault_id = T2.part_fault_id) GROUP BY T1.part_name ORDER BY fault_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of the three parts with the most recorded faults and their corresponding fault counts, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details FROM Assets AS T1 LEFT JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id WHERE T2.fault_log_entry_id IS NULL AND T1.maintenance_contract_id IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying assets that are covered by a maintenance contract but have never had a fault recorded, listing the asset ID and its details.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.company_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id AND T1.engineer_id < T2.engineer_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T3 ON T1.company_id = T3.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing pairs of maintenance engineers who work for the same third-party company, listing the names of both engineers and their shared company name.

sql: SELECT avg(part_count) FROM (SELECT count(part_id) AS part_count FROM Asset_Parts GROUP BY asset_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average number of parts per asset.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.skill_description FROM Skills AS T1 JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id JOIN Part_Faults AS T3 ON T2.part_fault_id = T3.part_fault_id JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T4 ON T3.part_fault_id = T4.part_fault_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T5 ON T4.fault_log_entry_id = T5.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Assets AS T6 ON T5.asset_id = T6.asset_id WHERE T6.supplier_company_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique skills required to fix faults on assets provided by the supplier with company ID 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Fault_Log AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Assets AS T4 ON T3.asset_id = T4.asset_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T5 ON T4.asset_id = T5.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T6 ON T5.part_id = T6.part_id WHERE T6.part_name = 'package' AND T6.chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique first and last names of maintenance engineers who have worked on assets that used a chargeable part named 'package'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T4 ON T3.part_fault_id = T4.part_fault_id JOIN Skills AS T5 ON T4.skill_id = T5.skill_id WHERE T5.skill_description IN ('Electrical', 'Mechanical') GROUP BY T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details HAVING count(DISTINCT T5.skill_id) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the asset ID and details for assets that have had faults requiring both 'Electrical' and 'Mechanical' skills for their repair.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 WHERE T1.engineer_id IN (SELECT engineer_id FROM Engineer_Skills) AND T1.engineer_id NOT IN (SELECT engineer_id FROM Engineer_Visits);

sql: SELECT T1.fault_log_entry_id, T1.fault_description FROM Fault_Log AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T2 ON T1.fault_log_entry_id = T2.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Part_Faults AS T3 ON T2.part_fault_id = T3.part_fault_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T4 ON T1.fault_log_entry_id = T4.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T5 ON T4.engineer_id = T5.engineer_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T6 ON T5.company_id = T6.company_id WHERE T3.fault_short_name = 'PW' AND T6.company_name = 'Schuppe-Carroll';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.line_1, T2.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T3.address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T1.person_id = 111;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T3.course_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(T2.course_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.student_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T4.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are attending the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id INTERSECT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of individuals who are simultaneously registered as both students and candidates.

sql: SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = '301' EXCEPT SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = '303';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the student IDs for students who are registered for course '301' but are not registered for course '303'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.attendance_count FROM People AS T1 JOIN (SELECT student_id, COUNT(*) AS attendance_count FROM Student_Course_Attendance GROUP BY student_id) AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first name, last name, and total attendance count for each student.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.line_1, T4.city, T4.country FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T5 ON T1.person_id = T5.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T5.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'French';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the full name and address details for all students who are registered for the 'French' course.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_id, T1.course_name HAVING COUNT(T2.student_id) > (SELECT AVG(reg_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(student_id) AS reg_count FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY course_id));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the course with a student enrollment count that is higher than the average enrollment across all courses.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 WHERE T2.student_id = T1.person_id);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of all people who are registered as students for at least one course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.student_details = 'Marry' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id WHERE T2.candidate_details = 'Robert';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of people who are either students with the detail 'Marry' or candidates with the detail 'Robert'.

sql: SELECT T1.email_address FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the email address for a candidate who failed an assessment.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.email_address FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T1.person_id IN (SELECT T3.person_id FROM People_Addresses AS T3 JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T4.state_province_county = 'Texas');

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 WHERE T1.person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations WHERE course_id = '301') AND T1.person_id NOT IN (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Course_Attendance WHERE course_id = '303');

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, (SELECT MAX(T3.date_from) FROM People_Addresses AS T3 WHERE T3.person_id = T1.person_id) AS most_recent_move_in FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id GROUP BY T1.person_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN (SELECT person_id FROM People_Addresses GROUP BY person_id HAVING COUNT(address_id) >= 1) AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each person's name alongside the name of the company where they are employed.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.People_ID) FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each company's name and the total number of people it employs.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T1.Age) FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Headquarters = 'USA' GROUP BY T3.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Industry = 'Oil and gas';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of people who work for a company in the 'Oil and gas' industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, SUM(T2.Year_working) FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each nationality alongside the total cumulative years of work experience for all people from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.People_ID) FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.People_ID) > 1;
