sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Year_working > 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an employee who has been working for more than 3 years.

sql: SELECT Industry, AVG(Sales_in_Billion) FROM company GROUP BY Industry;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each industry and the corresponding average sales in billions for that industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T1.Graduation_College = 'Iowa';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a person who graduated from 'Iowa' college along with the name of the company where they are employed.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'United States') AND Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the oldest person from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Sales_in_Billion > (SELECT AVG(Sales_in_Billion) FROM company);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who work for companies with sales greater than the average sales across all companies.

sql: SELECT Industry FROM company GROUP BY Industry HAVING COUNT(Company_ID) > 1 AND AVG(Market_Value_in_Billion) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the industries that contain more than one company and have an average market value greater than 150 billion.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.People_ID FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T1.Headquarters = 'USA');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and age of individuals who do not work for a company headquartered in the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.People_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the company that has the most employees.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM people) AND T3.Profits_in_Billion > 40;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality ORDER BY AVG(T2.Year_working) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single nationality with the highest average number of years worked.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of people who are either from the 'United States' or are employed in the 'Banking' industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of individuals from the United States who do not work in the banking industry.

sql: SELECT Name, Profits_in_Billion / Sales_in_Billion AS Profit_Ratio FROM company ORDER BY Profit_Ratio DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single company with the highest profit-to-sales ratio, listing the company name and the calculated ratio value.

sql: SELECT Graduation_College FROM people GROUP BY Graduation_College HAVING count(People_ID) >= 1 AND avg(Age) > 25;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the graduation colleges where the average age of their graduates is greater than 25.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'General Electric' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'United Kindom';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(T4.Age) FROM people AS T4 JOIN employment AS T5 ON T4.People_ID = T5.People_ID WHERE T5.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of a company along with the name of its oldest employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Graduation_College, COUNT(T1.People_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Graduation_College ORDER BY COUNT(T1.People_ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each graduation college along with the count of its employed alumni, ordered from the college with the most alumni to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM company AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM people AS T2 JOIN employment AS T3 ON T2.People_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T3.Company_ID = T1.Company_ID AND T2.Age < 26);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a company that employs at least one person younger than 26.

sql: SELECT Industry, SUM(Sales_in_Billion) AS Total_Sales FROM company GROUP BY Industry ORDER BY Total_Sales DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three industries by their total sales, ordered from highest to lowest sales.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T2.Year_working) FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T1.Age > 35 GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each company's name and the average years of employment for its employees who are over the age of 35.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Graduation_College = (SELECT Graduation_College FROM people WHERE Name = 'Reggie Lewis') AND Name != 'Reggie Lewis';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and age of another person who graduated from the same college as 'Reggie Lewis'.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID GROUP BY T3.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Nationality) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Year_working, AVG(T1.Market_Value_in_Billion) FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T2.Year_working;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the average market value (in billions) of companies, grouped by the number of years an employee has been working.

sql: SELECT Graduation_College FROM people GROUP BY Graduation_College HAVING MIN(Age) > 26;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the colleges where every recorded graduate from that institution is older than 26.

sql: SELECT * FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing every artist in the database along with their country of origin, gender, and preferred genre.

sql: SELECT g_name, rating FROM genre ORDER BY rating ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music genre and its corresponding rating, ordered from the lowest to highest rating.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE rating = 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and artist for all songs that have a specific rating of 8.

sql: SELECT * FROM genre WHERE most_popular_in = 'Bangladesh';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all details for music genres that are most popular in Bangladesh, including the genre name, its rating, and country of popularity.

sql: SELECT song_name, rating FROM song ORDER BY rating DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and rating of each song, ordered from the highest rating to the lowest.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE gender = 'Female';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all artists identified as female.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries represented by the artists.

sql: SELECT * FROM files WHERE formats = 'mp4';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all available details (ID, artist, size, duration, format) for files that are in the 'mp4' format.

sql: SELECT song_name, releasedate FROM song WHERE languages = 'bangla';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the song name and release date for all songs in the Bangla language.

sql: SELECT song_name, resolution FROM song WHERE resolution = 1080;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and resolutions for all songs available in 1080p resolution.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.rating, T2.artist_name, T2.gender FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each song's name and rating, along with the name and gender of the corresponding artist.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.f_id, T2.file_size FROM artist AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows that associate each artist with their corresponding file ID and file size.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist's name along with a count of their total number of songs.

sql: SELECT T1.country, AVG(T2.rating) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the average song rating for all artists from that country.

sql: SELECT genre_is, COUNT(*) FROM song GROUP BY genre_is;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music genre along with the total count of songs available for that genre.

sql: SELECT song_name, rating FROM song WHERE rating > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and ratings for all songs that have a rating greater than the average rating across all songs.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.file_size, T2.duration FROM song AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.f_id = T2.f_id WHERE T2.formats = 'mp3';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song name, file size, and duration for all songs available in the mp3 file format.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name, T2.artist_name FROM genre AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.g_name = T2.preferred_genre WHERE T2.country = 'India';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the preferred genre and name for each artist from India.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE releasedate = '28-AUG-2011';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the song name and artist for the song that was released on August 28, 2011.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE preferred_genre = 'tagore' UNION SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE preferred_genre = 'nazrul';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all artists whose preferred genre is either 'tagore' or 'nazrul'.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.country, T3.g_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN genre AS T3 ON T2.genre_is = T3.g_name WHERE T3.rating > 7;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and country of artists who perform in genres with a rating above 7, along with the name of that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.artist_name HAVING COUNT(T2.song_name) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist's name along with a total count of their songs for all artists with at least one song.

sql: SELECT g_name, rating FROM genre WHERE g_name IN (SELECT preferred_genre FROM artist WHERE country = 'Bangladesh');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and ratings of music genres that are preferred by artists from Bangladesh.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.artist_name = T1.artist_name AND T2.rating > 7);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who have at least one song with a rating greater than 7.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.gender, T3.g_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN genre AS T3 ON T2.genre_is = T3.g_name WHERE T1.gender = 'Female' AND T3.rating > 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing female artists and the names of the highly-rated genres (rating > 6) they are associated with.

sql: SELECT g_name FROM genre WHERE rating > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of genres whose ratings are higher than the overall average rating across all songs.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.artist_name NOT IN (SELECT T2.artist_name FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.rating < 6);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of artists who have never produced a song with a rating below 6.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.AvgRating FROM artist AS T1 JOIN (SELECT artist_name, AVG(rating) AS AvgRating FROM song GROUP BY artist_name) AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.AvgRating > 6;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artists whose average song rating is greater than 6, along with their specific calculated average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.rating FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.gender = 'Male' AND T1.rating = (SELECT MAX(rating) FROM song WHERE artist_name = 'Shrikanta');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song name and rating for all songs by male artists that have a rating equal to the highest-rated song by the artist 'Shrikanta'.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name FROM genre AS T1 WHERE T1.g_name IN (SELECT T2.genre_is FROM song AS T2 JOIN files AS T3 ON T2.f_id = T3.f_id WHERE T3.formats = 'mp3');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of music genres that have at least one song available in the 'mp3' format.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' AND T1.artist_name IN (SELECT T2.artist_name FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.genre_is = 'folk');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist from Bangladesh who also performs in the folk genre.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'India' INTERSECT SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE genre_is = 'tagore';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who is from India and has also performed a song in the 'tagore' genre.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.song_name, T3.file_size FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T2.f_id = T3.f_id WHERE T1.preferred_genre = T2.genre_is;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the artist name, song name, and file size for songs where the song's genre matches the artist's preferred genre.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name, COUNT(T2.song_name) FROM genre AS T1 LEFT JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.g_name = T2.genre_is GROUP BY T1.g_name HAVING COUNT(T2.song_name) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music genre that has one or more songs, along with the total count of songs for that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T1.gender FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.artist_name IN (SELECT T2.artist_name FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.rating > 7) AND T1.artist_name IN (SELECT T3.artist_name FROM files AS T3 WHERE T3.formats = 'mp4');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and gender of artists who have at least one song rated above 7 and also have files available in mp4 format.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.rating, T2.preferred_genre FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.rating >= (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM genre);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows for songs with a rating at or above the average rating of all genres, showing each song's name, its rating, and the artist's preferred genre.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name, country FROM song WHERE f_id IN (SELECT f_id FROM files WHERE duration > '3:00');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the song name, artist name, and country for songs with a duration longer than 3 minutes.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.num_songs FROM artist AS T1 JOIN (SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) as num_songs FROM song GROUP BY artist_name) AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name ORDER BY T2.num_songs DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist's name and the total number of songs they have, ordered from the artist with the most songs to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' AND T1.preferred_genre = T2.genre_is;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artists from Bangladesh who have recorded at least one song in their preferred genre.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' EXCEPT SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE genre_is = 'folk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of an artist from Bangladesh who does not perform folk music.

sql: SELECT * FROM city WHERE Status = 'Village';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all cities classified as 'Village', showing complete details for each including their area, population, and census ranking.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Total_Horses, Total_Cattle FROM farm WHERE Year = 1927;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the Farm ID and the total number of horses and cattle for that farm in the year 1927.

sql: SELECT Theme, Hosts FROM farm_competition WHERE Year = 2013;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the theme and the hosts for the farm competition held in the year 2013.

sql: SELECT Official_Name, Population FROM city ORDER BY Population DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing cities and their populations, ordered from the most populous to the least.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Pigs FROM farm WHERE Pigs > 4400;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing farms with more than 4400 pigs, showing each farm's ID and its total pig count.

sql: SELECT Competition_ID, Farm_ID, Rank FROM competition_record WHERE Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the first-place winner for each competition, listing the competition ID and the winning farm's ID.

sql: SELECT Official_Name, Area_km_2 FROM city WHERE Area_km_2 < 10;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official name and area for all cities with a land area smaller than 10 square kilometers.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Year FROM farm ORDER BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the distinct years for which farm data exists, sorted in chronological order.

sql: SELECT * FROM farm_competition WHERE Hosts = 'Alicia Keys';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the full details of the farm competition hosted by Alicia Keys.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Sheep_and_Goats FROM farm ORDER BY Sheep_and_Goats ASC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the farm IDs with the fewest sheep and goats, ordered from the lowest count to the highest.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T2.Official_Name FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the theme for each farm competition along with the official name of its corresponding host city.

sql: SELECT T2.Official_Name, COUNT(T1.Competition_ID) FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID GROUP BY T2.Official_Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the official name of each host city along with the total number of farm competitions held in that city.

sql: SELECT AVG(Total_Cattle), MAX(Pigs) FROM farm;
result_summary: Returns 1 row summarizing the average total number of cattle and the maximum number of pigs found on any single farm.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Total_Horses, T2.Rank FROM farm AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows that link each farm's competition rank with its corresponding year and total number of horses.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, COUNT(T2.Farm_ID) FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each competition theme along with a count of the total number of farms that participated in competitions under that theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T1.Year FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Status = 'Village';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the theme and year of farm competitions that were hosted in locations with a 'Village' status.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(Working_Horses), SUM(Oxen) FROM farm GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, with each row listing a year along with the total number of working horses and oxen for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name, T1.Population FROM city AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2006;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the official name and population of the city that hosted the farm competition in the year 2006.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record GROUP BY Farm_ID HAVING COUNT(Competition_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the IDs of farms that have participated in at least one competition.

sql: SELECT T2.Rank FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T1.Theme = 'Carnival M is back!';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ranks achieved by participants in the competition with the theme 'Carnival M is back!'.

sql: SELECT T3.Official_Name FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID JOIN city AS T3 ON T2.Host_city_ID = T3.City_ID WHERE T1.Farm_ID = 2 AND T1.Rank = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the official name of the host city for the competition where the farm with ID 2 achieved a rank of 2.

sql: SELECT Year, Total_Cattle FROM farm WHERE Total_Cattle > (SELECT AVG(Total_Cattle) FROM farm);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the year and total number of cattle for all years where the cattle count was higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme HAVING COUNT(T2.Farm_ID) >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the themes of farm competitions that have had participation from three or more farms.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Total_Horses FROM farm AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID WHERE T2.Rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the establishment year and total number of horses for each farm that has won a competition (achieved a rank of 1).

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name, T1.Population FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT T2.Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition AS T2 WHERE T2.Year < 2010);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official name and population of each city that hosted a farm competition before the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Official_Name FROM city AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM farm_competition AS T2 WHERE T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID AND T2.Hosts LIKE '%Wang%');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the official name of the city that has hosted a farm competition where at least one of the hosts has 'Wang' in their name.

sql: SELECT T4.Official_Name, T3.Theme, T2.Year, T1.Rank FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID JOIN farm_competition AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID JOIN city AS T4 ON T3.Host_city_ID = T4.City_ID;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows of historical farm competition records, showing the official name of the host city, the competition theme, the year of the event, and the rank achieved by each participating farm.

sql: SELECT Official_Name FROM city WHERE Status = 'Village' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Official_Name FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the official names of all cities that are classified as a 'Village' and have also hosted a farm competition.

sql: SELECT Theme FROM farm_competition EXCEPT SELECT T1.Theme FROM farm_competition AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_city_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Status = 'Town';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the themes of farm competitions that were not hosted in a city with the status of 'Town'.

sql: SELECT T2.Theme, T2.Year FROM farm_competition AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_city_ID = (SELECT City_ID FROM city ORDER BY Population DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the theme and year of any farm competition hosted in the city with the largest population.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Pigs FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Pigs > (SELECT AVG(T2.Pigs) FROM farm AS T2 JOIN competition_record AS T3 ON T2.Farm_ID = T3.Farm_ID WHERE T3.Rank = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the Farm ID and number of pigs for all farms that have more pigs than the average number found on first-place competition-winning farms.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, COUNT(T1.Farm_ID) FROM (SELECT T2.Year, T2.Farm_ID FROM farm AS T2 JOIN competition_record AS T3 ON T2.Farm_ID = T3.Farm_ID WHERE T3.Rank <= 3) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Year;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing, for each year, the total number of farms that achieved a top-3 rank in a competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year FROM farm AS T1 WHERE T1.Year = 1928 AND T1.Farm_ID IN (SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the Farm ID and Year for farms from 1928 that have also participated in a competition.

sql: SELECT Official_Name FROM city WHERE Status = 'Town' UNION SELECT Official_Name FROM city WHERE Area_km_2 > 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the official name of any city that is either classified as a 'Town' or has an area greater than 15 square kilometers.

sql: SELECT T1.Farm_ID, T1.Year, T1.Total_Cattle, (SELECT T2.Theme FROM farm_competition AS T2 WHERE T2.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID) FROM farm AS T1 JOIN competition_record AS T3 ON T1.Farm_ID = T3.Farm_ID WHERE T3.Rank = 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID, year, and total cattle count for farms that placed 3rd in a competition, along with the theme of that specific competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Host_city_ID, T1.Theme, T1.Hosts FROM farm_competition AS T1 WHERE T1.Competition_ID IN (SELECT Competition_ID FROM competition_record GROUP BY Competition_ID HAVING count(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the host city ID, theme, and hosts for farm competitions that have occurred more than once.

sql: SELECT Official_Name, Population, Area_km_2 FROM city WHERE City_ID IN (SELECT Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Year = 2013 OR Year = 2006);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the official name, population, and area for cities that hosted a farm competition in either 2006 or 2013.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Year, 'High Sheep' FROM farm WHERE Sheep_and_Goats > 8000 UNION SELECT Farm_ID, Year, 'High Pigs' FROM farm WHERE Pigs > 6000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying farms with either more than 8000 sheep and goats or more than 6000 pigs, including a category label to indicate which condition was met.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition GROUP BY Host_city_ID HAVING count(*) >= 1) AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average population for all cities that have hosted at least one farm competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Rank, T3.Theme FROM competition_record AS T1 JOIN farm AS T2 ON T1.Farm_ID = T2.Farm_ID JOIN farm_competition AS T3 ON T1.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T2.Year = 1928;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the rank achieved and the theme of competitions that farms from the year 1928 participated in.

sql: SELECT * FROM Products WHERE Product_Type_Code = 'Books';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all products classified as 'Books', showing each book's ID, type code, name, and price.

sql: SELECT address_details FROM Addresses WHERE Address_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the full address details for the specific address with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Product_Name, Product_Price FROM Products ORDER BY Product_Price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and price of each product, ordered from the most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT Other_Details FROM Locations;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing the 'Other_Details' for each location in the database.

sql: SELECT Event_Type_Code, COUNT(*) FROM Events GROUP BY Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each event type code and the total number of events corresponding to that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, T2.address_details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID WHERE T1.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the event type and full address details for all events classified as a 'Trade Show'.

sql: SELECT Party_Details FROM Parties WHERE Party_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the party details for the specific party identified by Party ID 3.

sql: SELECT * FROM Products WHERE Product_Price > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all product details for items with a price greater than $3000.

sql: SELECT T1.Role_Code, T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the role code and details for all parties that are designated as an 'Organizer'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Event_Type_Code FROM Events;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique event type code recorded in the events table.

sql: SELECT T2.Product_Name, COUNT(T1.Event_ID) FROM Products_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID GROUP BY T2.Product_Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each product name and the total number of events it is associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code, T2.address_details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID WHERE T1.Address_ID IN (1, 2, 3);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the event ID, event type, and full address details for all events taking place at three specific, pre-selected locations.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_Details, COUNT(T1.Event_ID) AS NumberOfEvents FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party_Details HAVING COUNT(T1.Event_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing political parties that have participated in more than one event, along with the total number of events for each party.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, T3.Product_Name FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T1.Event_Type_Code = 'Press Conferenc';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Event_ID FROM Assets_in_Events WHERE Asset_ID = 1 UNION SELECT Event_ID FROM Products_in_Events WHERE Product_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique IDs of events that feature either the asset with ID 1 or the product with ID 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, T3.Party_Details, T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS T3 ON T2.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows identifying the organizer for various events, listing the event's type code, the organizer's details, and the unique event ID.

sql: SELECT T2.Product_Name, T2.Product_Price FROM Products_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID WHERE T1.Event_ID IN (SELECT Event_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the product name and price for all products that were featured in 'Trade Show' events.

sql: SELECT T1.Other_Details, COUNT(T2.Asset_ID) FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Assets_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Asset_ID = T2.Asset_ID GROUP BY T1.Other_Details ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Asset_ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows that count the number of event appearances for each asset category, ordered from the most frequent to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code, T2.Other_Details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Finances AS T2 ON T1.Finance_ID = T2.Finance_ID WHERE T2.Other_Details = 'Good';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the event ID and type code for events that have a financial status of 'Good'.

sql: SELECT Event_ID FROM Agreements GROUP BY Event_ID HAVING COUNT(Document_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs for events that are associated with more than one document.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Participant';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the details of parties that have served in both 'Organizer' and 'Participant' roles in events.

sql: SELECT T3.Product_Name, T3.Product_Price FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T1.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show' ORDER BY T3.Product_Price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and price of the single most expensive product featured in any 'Trade Show' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T3.Product_Name = 'Special Dinning' UNION SELECT Event_ID FROM Assets_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID HAVING COUNT(Asset_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique IDs of events that either offer the 'Special Dinning' product or have at least one asset associated with them.

sql: SELECT T4.Other_Details, AVG(T3.Product_Price) AS Average_Product_Price FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID JOIN Locations AS T4 ON T1.Location_ID = T4.Location_ID GROUP BY T4.Other_Details;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows showing the average price of products for events, grouped by the 'Other Details' of the event location.

sql: SELECT Party_Details FROM Parties EXCEPT SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details for political parties that have never been assigned the role of 'Organizer' in any event.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Event_Type_Code, T4.address_details, T5.Other_Details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T1.Address_ID = T4.Address_ID JOIN Locations AS T5 ON T1.Location_ID = T5.Location_ID WHERE T3.Product_Price > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique event type, address, and location details for events that feature high-value products (priced over 3000).

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Party_ID) AS Unique_Participants FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Participant' GROUP BY T1.Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each event type and the total count of unique individuals who participated in that type of event.

sql: SELECT T3.address_details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.Address_ID = T3.Address_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID, T3.address_details HAVING COUNT(T2.Party_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the address details for events that had more than one party in attendance.

sql: SELECT Product_Name FROM Products EXCEPT SELECT T3.Product_Name FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T1.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of products that have never been featured in a 'Trade Show' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, SUM(T3.Product_Price) AS Total_Product_Value FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID ORDER BY Total_Product_Value DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each event's ID and the total value of all products associated with it, ordered from the highest total value event to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Assets_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Assets AS T3 ON T2.Asset_ID = T3.Asset_ID JOIN Finances AS T4 ON T1.Finance_ID = T4.Finance_ID WHERE T3.Other_Details = 'Transportation Cars' AND T4.Other_Details = 'Good';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the event ID and type code for events that utilized 'Transportation Cars' and were categorized with a 'Good' financial status.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Parties AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Participant';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, AVG(T1.Agreement_Count) FROM (SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, COUNT(T2.Document_ID) AS Agreement_Count FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Agreements AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code) AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each event type code along with the average number of agreements generated for events of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T3 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.Product_ID WHERE T2.address_details LIKE '%VT %' AND T4.Product_Type_Code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the event ID and type for events located in Vermont that feature 'Food' products.

sql: SELECT T2.Other_Details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID GROUP BY T2.Other_Details HAVING COUNT(T1.Event_ID) > (SELECT AVG(EventCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Event_ID) AS EventCount FROM Events GROUP BY Location_ID) AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details for locations that have hosted a higher-than-average number of events.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Party_Details FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS T3 ON T1.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T2.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T5.Product_Price > (SELECT AVG(Product_Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique details of parties that organized events featuring products priced above the average of all products.

sql: SELECT T3.Channel_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Channels AS T3 ON T1.Channel_ID = T3.Channel_ID GROUP BY T3.Channel_ID ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Product_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for the channel that featured the highest number of distinct products across all its events.

sql: SELECT T3.Party_Details, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Event_ID) AS EventsOrganized, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.Asset_ID) AS UniqueAssetsUsed FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS T3 ON T1.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID LEFT JOIN Assets_in_Events AS T4 ON T2.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'Organizer' GROUP BY T3.Party_Details;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each party that has acted as an organizer, along with a count of the unique events they organized and the total number of unique assets used across all of those events.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T3.Product_Type_Code = 'Books' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T3.Product_Type_Code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for the event that featured products of both 'Books' and 'Food' types.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Events AS T1 LEFT JOIN Agreements AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID LEFT JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T3 ON T1.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID HAVING COUNT(T3.Party_ID) > 0 ORDER BY CAST(COUNT(T2.Document_ID) AS REAL) / COUNT(T3.Party_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single event with the highest ratio of associated documents to participating parties, considering only events with at least one party.

sql: SELECT * FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all available information for swimmers from Australia, including their name, nationality, and times for various distances.

sql: SELECT name, Capacity FROM stadium ORDER BY Capacity DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and capacity of each stadium, ordered from the largest to the smallest capacity.

sql: SELECT Name FROM event WHERE Year = '2012';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the event that occurred in the year 2012.

sql: SELECT * FROM record WHERE Result = 'Win';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all winning competition records, showing the record ID, result, swimmer ID, and event ID for each win.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM swimmer;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all swimmers in the database.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Nationality FROM swimmer;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each unique nationality represented by the swimmers in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, Opening_year FROM stadium WHERE City = 'Baku';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and opening year for stadiums located in 'Baku'.

sql: SELECT * FROM swimmer WHERE name = 'Craig Stevens';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete record for the swimmer named 'Craig Stevens', detailing their nationality and performance times at various distances.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Year FROM event;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique year in which an event occurred.

sql: SELECT name FROM stadium WHERE Opening_year = 2005;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all stadiums that opened in the year 2005.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Result FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing the name of each swimmer alongside their corresponding result for every competition record.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.ID) FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each stadium's name along with the total count of events held there.

sql: SELECT name FROM swimmer WHERE ID IN (SELECT Swimmer_ID FROM record WHERE Result = 'Win');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all swimmers who have recorded at least one win.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Capacity FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'FINA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and capacity of the stadium that hosted the 'FINA' event.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(*) FROM swimmer GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each nationality and the total number of swimmers from that nationality.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of events that have at least one associated record.

sql: SELECT name, Time FROM swimmer WHERE meter_100 < 57.5;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and a recorded race time for each swimmer who completed the 100-meter event in under 57.5 seconds.

sql: SELECT MIN(Opening_year) FROM stadium;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the opening year of the oldest stadium.

sql: SELECT name, Nationality FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality IN ('Italy', 'Poland');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and nationalities of swimmers from either Italy or Poland.

sql: SELECT Name FROM event WHERE Stadium_ID IN (SELECT ID FROM stadium WHERE Capacity > 70000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an event that takes place in a very large capacity stadium (over 70,000).

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T1.City, T1.Capacity FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID JOIN record AS T3 ON T2.ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN swimmer AS T4 ON T3.Swimmer_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Nationality = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique stadiums (name, city, and capacity) that have hosted an event where an Australian swimmer set a record.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Event_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of swimmers who have competed in more than one distinct event.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Year) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Win' AND T3.Name = 'Pacific';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the swimmers who won the 'Pacific' event.

sql: SELECT name FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality = 'Poland' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Loss';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the unique names of swimmers who are either from Poland or have a recorded loss.

sql: SELECT name FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality = 'Italy' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Win';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT avg(T.Capacity) FROM (SELECT Capacity FROM stadium WHERE Opening_year > 2004) AS T;
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the average capacity of all stadiums that opened after the year 2004.

sql: SELECT Nationality FROM swimmer GROUP BY Nationality ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single nationality with the most swimmers, determined by the highest count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Spain';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of events held in stadiums located in Spain.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T1.Nationality FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Capacity < 70000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names and nationalities of swimmers who have competed in events held at stadiums with a capacity of less than 70,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Name = 'World Master' AND T1.Nationality != 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all non-Australian swimmers who have competed in the 'World Master' event.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the stadium that hosts the highest number of events.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Year = '2016' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Year = '2012';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of swimmers who participated in events in both the year 2012 and the year 2016.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Nationality) FROM event AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN swimmer AS T3 ON T2.Swimmer_ID = T3.ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each event name and the corresponding count of unique nationalities of swimmers who have set records in that event.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Opening_year = 2005;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing swimmers and the events they participated in, specifically for those events held in stadiums that opened in the year 2005.

sql: SELECT avg(c) FROM (SELECT count(Swimmer_ID) AS c FROM record GROUP BY Event_ID) AS T;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of swimmers participating in an event.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM record AS T2 WHERE T2.Swimmer_ID = T1.ID AND T2.Result NOT IN ('Win', 'NC'));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing the names of swimmers whose competition records consist exclusively of 'Win' or 'NC' (No Contest) results.

sql: SELECT name, Nationality, meter_100, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Nationality ORDER BY meter_100) FROM swimmer;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each swimmer's name, nationality, and 100-meter time, along with a calculated rank of their 100-meter performance within their respective country.

sql: SELECT Country FROM stadium EXCEPT SELECT Nationality FROM swimmer;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the countries that have a stadium but are not represented by any swimmer's nationality in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Name, T2.Year FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Stadium_ID WHERE T2.Year = (SELECT MAX(Year) FROM event AS T3 WHERE T3.Stadium_ID = T1.ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each stadium, along with the name and year of the most recent event held there.

sql: SELECT * FROM city WHERE City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all recorded details for the city of 'Shanghai', including its ID, Hanzi and Pinyin names, regional population, and GDP.

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population FROM city ORDER BY Regional_Population DESC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each city and its corresponding regional population, ordered from the most to least populous region.

sql: SELECT Venue, Competition FROM match WHERE Competition = 'Friendly match';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the venue and competition type for all matches that were 'Friendly match' competitions.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM city;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value: the total count of all cities in the database.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Competition FROM match;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of each unique competition type found in the match data.

sql: SELECT Year, Match_ID FROM hosting_city WHERE Year = 2008;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the year and Match ID for the event hosted in 2008.

sql: SELECT City_ID FROM temperature WHERE Jan = 17.8;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID for the city that had a January temperature of exactly 17.8.

sql: SELECT Score, Result FROM match ORDER BY Match_ID ASC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the score and result for the first three recorded matches, ordered by their match ID from lowest to highest.

sql: SELECT City, GDP FROM city WHERE GDP > 700;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and GDP for cities with a GDP greater than 700.

sql: SELECT City_ID, (Jan + Feb + Mar) / 3 AS Q1_Avg_Temp FROM temperature;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each city's ID alongside its calculated average temperature for the first quarter of the year (January-March).

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP, T2.Year FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and GDP for each city that has hosted an event, along with the year it hosted.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Competition, T2.Year FROM match AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, showing the date and competition for each match, along with its associated hosting year.

sql: SELECT Competition, COUNT(*) FROM match GROUP BY Competition;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each competition along with the total count of matches held within it.

sql: SELECT T1.City, COUNT(T2.Match_ID) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city along with the total number of matches it has hosted.

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(*) FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each year along with the total number of hosting cities for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T2.Jan, T2.Jul FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Jan > 18;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing cities with a January temperature greater than 18 degrees, showing each city's specific January and July temperatures.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Venue FROM match AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the date and venue for the match that took place in the year 2009.

sql: SELECT AVG(Regional_Population), AVG(GDP) FROM city;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average regional population and average Gross Domestic Product (GDP) across all cities.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Match_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the city that hosted the specific match with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T2.Year, T2.Match_ID FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T1.City = 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the year and ID for each match hosted in the city of 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )'.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T2.Year, T3.Competition, T3.Score FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows detailing football matches, providing the host city, the year it was played, the competition name, and the final score for each event.

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population FROM city WHERE Regional_Population > (SELECT AVG(Regional_Population) FROM city);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying cities with a regional population greater than the average across all cities, listing each city's name and its population.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City HAVING COUNT(T2.Match_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that has hosted more than one match.

sql: SELECT T3.Competition FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T1.City = 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )' GROUP BY T3.Competition;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct competition hosted in the city of 'Nanjing (Jiangsu)'.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City AND T2.Year = 2008);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the city that hosted an event in the year 2008.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Venue FROM match AS T1 WHERE T1.Match_ID IN (SELECT T2.Match_ID FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_City IN (SELECT T3.City_ID FROM city AS T3 WHERE T3.GDP > 1000));
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the date and venue for a match that was held in a city with a GDP greater than 1000.

sql: SELECT City FROM city WHERE City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city WHERE Year = 2009) INTERSECT SELECT City FROM city WHERE City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city WHERE Year = 2010);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city that hosted an event in both the year 2009 and the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City EXCEPT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all cities that have served as a host, with the explicit exclusion of Shanghai.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' UNION SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique cities that hosted matches for either the '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' or a 'Friendly match'.

sql: SELECT City FROM city WHERE City_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of cities that have never hosted a 'Friendly match'.

sql: SELECT T3.Competition, AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T3.Competition;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each competition along with the average GDP of the cities that hosted matches for that competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, MAX(T2.Regional_Population) FROM hosting_city AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_City = T2.City_ID GROUP BY T1.Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each hosting year along with the maximum regional population of a host city for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Host_City = T1.City_ID) = (SELECT MAX(cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Host_City));
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city that has hosted the greatest number of events.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.Hanzi, T3.Jul FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T3 ON T1.City_ID = T3.City_ID WHERE T3.Jul > 23 AND T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying host cities with a July temperature above 23 degrees, listing each city's name, Hanzi name, and July temperature.

sql: SELECT T1.Competition FROM match AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID GROUP BY T1.Competition HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Year) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a competition that was hosted across more than one distinct year.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Jul) FROM temperature AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT T2.City_ID FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.Regional_Population < 10000000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average July temperature for all cities located in regions with a population of less than 10 million.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city) AND T1.GDP < (SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city WHERE City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and GDP of host cities whose GDP is below the average GDP of all host cities.

sql: SELECT T1.Venue, T1.Date FROM match AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Match_ID, Year FROM hosting_city WHERE Year > 2008) AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the venue and date for matches whose associated hosting event took place in a year after 2008.

sql: SELECT T1.City, (T2.Jan+T2.Feb+T2.Mar+T2.Apr+T2.Jun+T2.Jul+T2.Aug+T2.Sep+T2.Oct+T2.Nov+T2.Dec)/11.0 FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each host city and its average temperature, calculated across 11 specified months of the year.

sql: SELECT Year FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Year ORDER BY COUNT(Match_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year that hosted the most matches.

sql: SELECT * FROM airlines WHERE Country = 'USA';

sql: SELECT * FROM airports WHERE City = 'Aberdeen ';

result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing all flights operated by the airline with ID 1, showing the flight number and the source and destination airports for each route.

sql: SELECT AirportName, City FROM airports WHERE Country = 'United States ';
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the name and city of airports located in the United States.

sql: SELECT Airline, Abbreviation FROM airlines;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each airline's full name and its corresponding abbreviation.

sql: SELECT * FROM flights WHERE FlightNo = 28;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the airline, source airport, and destination airport for all flights with the specific flight number 28.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM airlines;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique country associated with the airlines in the dataset.

sql: SELECT AirportName FROM airports WHERE AirportCode = 'ABR';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the airport corresponding to the specific airport code 'ABR'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM airlines;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value, which is the total count of all airlines.

sql: SELECT * FROM flights WHERE SourceAirport = ' APG';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing all flight details, including airline, flight number, source, and destination, for flights departing from the source airport 'APG'.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline, COUNT(T2.FlightNo) FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each airline and the total number of flights it operates.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode;
result_summary: Returns 1200 rows, listing each flight number along with the full name of its corresponding source airport.

sql: SELECT T1.FlightNo, T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode;
result_summary: Returns 1200 rows listing each flight number alongside the full name of its destination airport.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline WHERE T2.SourceAirport = ' APG';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the distinct airlines that operate flights originating from the source airport 'APG'.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(AirportCode) FROM airports GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 85 rows listing each city along with the total count of airports located in it.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline, T1.Abbreviation FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline WHERE T2.FlightNo = 44;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the full name and abbreviation for all airlines that operate flight number 44.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName, T2.City FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T1.Airline = 1;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the name and city for each destination airport served by the airline with ID 1.

sql: SELECT T2.SourceAirport, T2.DestAirport FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline WHERE T1.Abbreviation = 'UAL';
result_summary: Returns 100 rows listing the source and destination airports for all flights operated by the airline with the abbreviation 'UAL'.

sql: SELECT City FROM airports GROUP BY City HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of cities that have more than one airport.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of airlines that have more than one flight recorded.

sql: SELECT Airline FROM airlines WHERE uid NOT IN (SELECT T1.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T2.City = 'Aberdeen ');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the airlines that do not operate any flights departing from the city of Aberdeen.

sql: SELECT T1.SourceAirport, T1.DestAirport FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.SourceAirport = T2.DestAirport AND T1.DestAirport = T2.SourceAirport WHERE T1.SourceAirport < T2.DestAirport;

error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T2.DestAirport) = T3.AirportCode GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Country) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline ORDER BY COUNT(T2.FlightNo) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the airline that has the most flights, determined by counting the total number of flights for each airline.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline, T3.AirportName, T4.AirportName FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T2.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T2.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T3.Country = 'United States ';
result_summary: Returns 1200 rows listing the airline name, source airport name, and destination airport name for flights that depart from airports within the United States.

sql: SELECT T3.City, T4.City FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.uid JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T1.FlightNo = 28 AND T2.Airline = 'United Airlines';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the departure and arrival cities for United Airlines flight number 28.

sql: SELECT Airline, FlightNo FROM flights WHERE TRIM(DestAirport) IN (SELECT AirportCode FROM airports WHERE City = 'Abilene ');
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing the airline and flight number for all flights destined for an airport in the city of Abilene.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T1.Airline = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode WHERE T1.Airline = 2;
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the names of destination airports that are served by both Airline 1 and Airline 2.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Airline, T4.Airline FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.DestAirport = T2.DestAirport JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T2.Airline = T4.uid WHERE T3.Airline < T4.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 66 rows, listing every unique pair of airlines that share at least one common destination airport.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.AirportName HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT avg(cnt) FROM (SELECT count(*) AS cnt FROM flights GROUP BY DestAirport));
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the names of destination airports that have a higher-than-average number of incoming flights.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline, T1.FlightNo FROM flights AS T1 WHERE T1.FlightNo = (SELECT MIN(T2.FlightNo) FROM flights AS T2 WHERE T2.Airline = T1.Airline);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, identifying the lowest flight number for each distinct airline.

sql: SELECT Airport FROM (SELECT TRIM(SourceAirport) AS Airport FROM flights UNION ALL SELECT TRIM(DestAirport) AS Airport FROM flights) AS T1 GROUP BY Airport ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top three busiest airports, ranked in descending order by their total number of departures and arrivals.

sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline WHERE T1.Country = 'USA' GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING COUNT(T2.FlightNo) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of U.S. airlines that have more than two recorded flights in the system.

sql: SELECT Airline FROM airlines AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flights AS T2 WHERE T2.Airline = T1.uid);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of all airlines that have at least one corresponding flight record.

sql: SELECT T3.AirportName, T4.AirportName FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline JOIN airports AS T3 ON TRIM(T2.SourceAirport) = T3.AirportCode JOIN airports AS T4 ON TRIM(T2.DestAirport) = T4.AirportCode WHERE T1.Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 1200 rows listing the origin and destination airport names for all flights operated by US-based airlines.

sql: SELECT SourceAirport, DestAirport FROM flights GROUP BY SourceAirport, DestAirport ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the source and destination airports for the single busiest flight route, determined by the total number of flights.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.AirportName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Airline) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 89 rows listing the names of destination airports that are serviced by more than one distinct airline.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT T2.City FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode GROUP BY T2.City HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Airline) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count (85) of source airport cities that are served by more than one distinct airline.

sql: SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = T2.AirportCode UNION SELECT T2.AirportName FROM flights AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = T2.AirportCode;
result_summary: Returns 89 rows listing the unique names of all airports that served as either a source or a destination in the flights data.

sql: SELECT * FROM county;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all available counties along with their corresponding ID, name, population, and zip code.

sql: SELECT * FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the political parties and their slate of candidates for major offices in the 1998 election.

sql: SELECT Delegate, Committee FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Appropriations';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all delegates who serve on the 'Appropriations' committee.

sql: SELECT County_name, Zip_code FROM county WHERE Population = 90000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the county name and zip code for any county with a population of exactly 90,000.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Committee FROM election;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each unique committee involved in an election.

sql: SELECT Governor, Year FROM party WHERE Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of governors and the corresponding year for those belonging to the 'Democratic' party.

sql: SELECT Delegate FROM election WHERE First_Elected = 2002.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of delegates who were first elected in the year 2002.

sql: SELECT County_name, Population FROM county WHERE Zip_code = 'D21';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and population of the county associated with the zip code 'D21'.

sql: SELECT County_name, Population FROM county ORDER BY Population DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each county's name and population, ordered from most to least populous.

sql: SELECT Delegate FROM election WHERE District = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of the delegates representing District 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.Party FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each delegate's name alongside their corresponding political party.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.County_name FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows that match each delegate with the name of the county they represent.

sql: SELECT Committee, COUNT(Delegate) FROM election GROUP BY Committee;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each committee along with the total count of delegates serving on it.

sql: SELECT T2.County_name, T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id WHERE T1.Committee = 'Economic Matters';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the delegate from the 'Economic Matters' committee and their corresponding county name.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.Governor, T2.Year FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each delegate along with the governor and year associated with their political party.

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(*) FROM party GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each year along with the total number of parties held in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id WHERE T2.Population > 21000.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the delegates who represent counties with a population greater than 21,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all delegates from the 'Democratic' party.

sql: SELECT avg(Population) FROM county;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average population across all counties.

sql: SELECT US_Senate FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0 AND Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the US Senator for the Democratic party in the year 1998.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T3.Party, T2.County_name FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id JOIN party AS T3 ON T1.Party = T3.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each delegate's name, their political party, and the county they represent.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate, T2.County_name FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id WHERE T2.Population > (SELECT AVG(Population) FROM county);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the delegate and county name for all delegates who represent a county with an above-average population.

sql: SELECT Committee FROM election GROUP BY Committee HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the committee that is associated with more than one election record.

sql: SELECT Party FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0 EXCEPT SELECT Party FROM party WHERE Year = 2002.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the political party that was active in 1998 but no longer active in 2002.

sql: SELECT T1.Party FROM party AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM election AS T2 WHERE T2.Party = T1.Party_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of political parties that have participated in at least one election.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.pop) FROM (SELECT T3.Population as pop FROM election AS T2 JOIN county AS T3 ON T2.District = T3.County_Id WHERE T2.Committee = 'Appropriations') AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average population of counties represented on the 'Appropriations' committee.

sql: SELECT Comptroller FROM party INTERSECT SELECT Attorney_General FROM party;

sql: SELECT T2.Party, COUNT(T1.Delegate) FROM election AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party HAVING COUNT(T1.Delegate) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each political party that has at least one delegate, along with the total number of delegates for that party.

sql: SELECT Delegate, First_Elected FROM election WHERE First_Elected IN (SELECT Year FROM party WHERE Attorney_General = 'Eliot Spitzer');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and first election years of delegates who were initially elected in a year when Eliot Spitzer was the Attorney General.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id JOIN party AS T3 ON T1.Party = T3.Party_ID WHERE T2.County_name = 'Howard' AND T3.Party = 'Democratic';

sql: SELECT Governor FROM party UNION SELECT Lieutenant_Governor FROM party;

sql: SELECT County_name FROM county WHERE County_Id NOT IN (SELECT District FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Appropriations');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of counties that do not have any elections associated with the 'Appropriations' committee.

sql: SELECT T3.Party, SUM(T2.Population) FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id JOIN party AS T3 ON T1.Party = T3.Party_ID GROUP BY T3.Party;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, showing the total population for each political party by summing the populations of their associated election districts.

sql: SELECT Delegate, First_Elected FROM election WHERE First_Elected = (SELECT MAX(First_Elected) FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the delegate with the most recent "first elected" year, showing their name and that specific year.

sql: SELECT T1.County_name, T2.Delegate_Count FROM county AS T1 JOIN (SELECT District, COUNT(*) AS Delegate_Count FROM election GROUP BY District) AS T2 ON T1.County_Id = T2.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each county's name and the total number of delegates associated with that county, based on election data.

sql: SELECT Delegate FROM election EXCEPT SELECT Delegate FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Economic Matters';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Governor FROM party AS T1 WHERE T1.Party_ID IN (SELECT T2.Party FROM election AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of governors whose political parties have participated in an election.

sql: SELECT Year FROM party GROUP BY Year HAVING count(Party_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the years in which more than one party occurred.

sql: SELECT T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id WHERE T2.Population = (SELECT MAX(Population) FROM county);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the delegates who serve the county with the largest population.

sql: SELECT T2.County_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Party) FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id GROUP BY T2.County_name;

sql: SELECT * FROM Manufacturers;
