sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Manufacturers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all manufacturers.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the average price of all products made by that manufacturer.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price > 150;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of manufacturers that produce products costing more than $150.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T2.Name = 'Sony';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price for products manufactured by Sony.

sql: SELECT T2.Headquarter, COUNT(T1.Code) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Headquarter;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing the total count of products for each manufacturer headquarter location.

sql: SELECT Headquarter, SUM(Revenue) FROM Manufacturers GROUP BY Headquarter;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each manufacturer headquarter and the corresponding total revenue generated from that location.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, MAX(T1.Price), MIN(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the maximum and minimum price of the products they offer.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T1.Price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T2.Revenue = 120.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the manufacturer's name, product name, and price for products made by a manufacturer with a revenue of exactly 120.0.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers who offer more than one product.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the manufacturer that produces the highest-priced product.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and price of products that cost more than the average price of all products.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING AVG(T1.Price) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers whose products have an average price greater than 150.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Manufacturers WHERE Code NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Manufacturer FROM Products);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Price < (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM Products) ORDER BY Price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and price of the second-most expensive product in the catalog.

sql: SELECT Name, Revenue FROM Manufacturers WHERE Revenue < (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and revenue of manufacturers whose revenue is less than the price of the single most expensive product available.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Manufacturer IN (SELECT Code FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Tokyo');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of a product from a manufacturer headquartered in Tokyo.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Code) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the manufacturer that produces the most products.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING AVG(T1.Price) > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing manufacturers whose average product price is greater than the overall average, showing each manufacturer's name and their specific average price.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Manufacturers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.Manufacturer = T1.Code AND T2.Price > 200) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS T3 WHERE T3.Manufacturer = T1.Code AND T3.Price < 150);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a manufacturer that sells both high-priced products (over 200) and low-priced products (under 150).

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Manufacturers AS T1 WHERE T1.Code IN (SELECT Manufacturer FROM Products) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.Manufacturer = T1.Code AND T2.Price <= 150);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of manufacturers whose entire product line consists of items with a price greater than 150.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Code) FROM Manufacturers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Manufacturer GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the total count of products they produce.

sql: SELECT Founder FROM Manufacturers WHERE Code = (SELECT Manufacturer FROM Products WHERE Name = 'Hard drive');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the founder of the company that manufactures the 'Hard drive' product.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T2.Headquarter = 'Tokyo' UNION SELECT Name FROM Products WHERE Price > 200;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of products that are either made by a manufacturer headquartered in Tokyo or have a price greater than 200.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Los Angeles';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of manufacturers that have products, excluding any manufacturers headquartered in Los Angeles.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T1.Price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code JOIN (SELECT Manufacturer, MAX(Price) AS max_p FROM Products GROUP BY Manufacturer) AS T3 ON T1.Manufacturer = T3.Manufacturer AND T1.Price = T3.max_p;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying the most expensive product from each manufacturer, listing the manufacturer's name, the product's name, and its price.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, T1.Price - T3.avg_price AS price_difference FROM Products AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Manufacturer, AVG(Price) AS avg_price FROM Products GROUP BY Manufacturer) AS T3 ON T1.Manufacturer = T3.Manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each product's name and price, along with the calculated difference between its price and the average price of all products from the same manufacturer.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING SUM(T1.Price) > 300;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing manufacturers whose products have a combined price over 300, showing each manufacturer's name and the total product price sum.

sql: SELECT AVG(Revenue) FROM Manufacturers WHERE Code IN (SELECT Manufacturer FROM Products WHERE Price > 150);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average revenue for all manufacturers that produce at least one product with a price greater than 150.

sql: SELECT Venue, sum(Num_of_Audience) FROM debate GROUP BY Venue;

sql: SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T2.Name, T3.Name FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Affirmative = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Negative = T3.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each debate by its ID, along with the names of the participants on the affirmative and negative sides for that debate.

sql: SELECT If_Affirmative_Win, count(*) FROM debate_people GROUP BY If_Affirmative_Win;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows tallying the total number of debate participants, grouped by whether the affirmative side won ('T') or lost ('F').

sql: SELECT T3.Venue, T3.Date FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Affirmative JOIN debate AS T3 ON T2.Debate_ID = T3.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID WHERE T1.Party = 'Republican' AND T4.Party = 'Republican';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the venue and date for any debate where both the affirmative and negative participants were from the Republican party.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM (SELECT Affirmative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people) AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Person_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Person_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the person who has participated in the most debates, based on counting their appearances on both the affirmative and negative sides.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Name FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T3.People_ID = T2.Affirmative OR T3.People_ID = T2.Negative WHERE T1.Num_of_Audience > (SELECT avg(Num_of_Audience) FROM debate);

sql: SELECT Party FROM people GROUP BY Party ORDER BY avg(Age) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T3.Name FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Affirmative = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Negative = T3.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of the participants for each debate, identifying the speaker for the affirmative position and the speaker for the negative position.

sql: SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T1.Venue, abs(T3.Age - T4.Age) AS age_diff FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID ORDER BY age_diff DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the single debate with the largest age gap between its affirmative and negative participants, listing the debate ID, venue, and that age difference.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people UNION SELECT Negative FROM debate_people) AND T1.People_ID NOT IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'F' UNION SELECT Negative FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'T');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of people who have participated in debates and have never lost, effectively identifying debaters with a perfect winning record.

sql: SELECT avg(T2.Age) FROM (SELECT Affirmative AS pid FROM debate_people WHERE Debate_ID IN (SELECT Debate_ID FROM debate WHERE Venue = 'Manama , Bahrain') UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS pid FROM debate_people WHERE Debate_ID IN (SELECT Debate_ID FROM debate WHERE Venue = 'Manama , Bahrain')) AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.pid = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age of all people who participated in debates held at the 'Manama , Bahrain' venue.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Affirmative JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Negative = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Party <> T3.Party UNION SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Negative JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Party <> T3.Party;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of people who have participated in a debate against a member of a different political party.

sql: SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T1.Venue FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Age < 40 AND T4.Age < 40;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID and venue for debates where both the affirmative and negative participants are under the age of 40.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID OR T2.Negative = T3.People_ID GROUP BY T3.People_ID ORDER BY count(DISTINCT T1.Venue) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the person who has debated in the most distinct venues.

sql: SELECT Party, count(*) FROM (SELECT T1.Party FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Affirmative WHERE T2.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' UNION ALL SELECT T1.Party FROM people AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Negative WHERE T2.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F') AS T3 GROUP BY Party;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Affirmative AS People_ID FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'F' UNION SELECT Negative AS People_ID FROM debate_people WHERE If_Affirmative_Win = 'T') AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.District = 'New York 1';

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM people AS T3 WHERE T3.People_ID IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN debate AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'October 21, 2011' UNION SELECT Negative FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN debate AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'October 21, 2011' EXCEPT SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN debate AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'December 17, 2014' UNION SELECT Negative FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN debate AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'December 17, 2014');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who participated in a debate on October 21, 2011, but did not participate in the debate on December 17, 2014.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Party = 'Republican' AND T4.Party = 'Democratic';

sql: SELECT T2.Age FROM (SELECT Affirmative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people) AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Person_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Age ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common age among all people who have participated in a debate.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Debate_ID, T1.Venue, T1.Date FROM debate AS T1 JOIN debate_people AS T2 ON T1.Debate_ID = T2.Debate_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Affirmative = T3.People_ID JOIN people AS T4 ON T2.Negative = T4.People_ID WHERE T3.Age > (SELECT avg(Age) FROM people) OR T4.Age > (SELECT avg(Age) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique ID, venue, and date for debates where at least one of the participants was older than the average age of all people.

sql: SELECT CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM people p JOIN debate_people dp ON (p.People_ID = dp.Affirmative AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T') OR (p.People_ID = dp.Negative AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F') WHERE p.Party = 'Democratic') AS REAL) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM people p JOIN (SELECT Affirmative AS pid FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS pid FROM debate_people) AS T ON p.People_ID = T.pid WHERE p.Party = 'Democratic');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the debate win rate for the Democratic party, calculated by dividing their total wins by their total debate participations.

sql: SELECT AVG(T3.p_age), AVG(T3.f_age) FROM (SELECT T1.age AS p_age, T2.age AS f_age FROM PersonFriend AS PF JOIN Person AS T1 ON PF.name = T1.name JOIN Person AS T2 ON PF.friend = T2.name) AS T3;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Height FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of climbers from 'West Germany' alongside the height of the mountain they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Climber_ID) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain's name along with the total number of climbers recorded for that mountain.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Points) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Tanzania' GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each mountain in Tanzania along with the average points scored by climbers on it.

sql: SELECT Country, SUM(Points) FROM climber GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country along with the total points accumulated by climbers from that country.

sql: SELECT Range, MAX(Height) FROM mountain GROUP BY Range;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each mountain range along with the height of its highest peak.

sql: SELECT Range FROM mountain GROUP BY Range HAVING COUNT(Mountain_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of mountain ranges that contain more than one mountain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Prominence > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of climbers who have climbed mountains with a prominence greater than 1000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of climbers who have climbed the mountain 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)'.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) FROM mountain GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the total count of mountains located in it.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber WHERE Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and points for each climber with a point score higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Mountain_ID FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and height for each mountain that has at least one associated climber record.

sql: SELECT Name FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania' AND Mountain_ID IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of mountains located in Tanzania that have at least one recorded climber.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T2.Height = (SELECT MAX(Height) FROM mountain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and country of each climber who has ascended the tallest mountain.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(T1.Climber_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of mountains that have been climbed by more than one person.

sql: SELECT Country FROM climber UNION SELECT Country FROM mountain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique countries that are home to either a climber or a mountain.

sql: SELECT Name FROM mountain WHERE Mountain_ID NOT IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all mountains that have no recorded climbers.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, MAX(T1.Points) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain by name, along with the highest point score achieved by any climber on that specific mountain.

sql: SELECT Country FROM mountain WHERE Prominence = (SELECT MAX(Prominence) FROM mountain);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country that contains the mountain with the greatest prominence.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Height) FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average height of mountains that have been climbed by individuals from 'West Germany'.

sql: SELECT Name, Points - (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber) AS Point_Difference FROM climber;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each climber's name and the calculated difference between their points and the average points of all climbers.

sql: SELECT Range FROM mountain GROUP BY Range HAVING AVG(Height) > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of mountain ranges where the average height of all mountains within that range exceeds 5000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM mountain AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber AS T2 WHERE T2.Mountain_ID = T1.Mountain_ID AND T2.Country = 'West Germany');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of mountains that have been climbed by at least one person from 'West Germany'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Points, T2.Name FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID WHERE T1.Points = (SELECT MAX(T3.Points) FROM climber AS T3 WHERE T3.Mountain_ID = T1.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the climber with the highest point score for each mountain, showing the climber's name, their points, and the corresponding mountain's name.

sql: SELECT T2.Range FROM climber AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID GROUP BY T2.Range ORDER BY SUM(T1.Points) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the mountain range where climbers have accumulated the most total points.

sql: SELECT AVG(Prominence) FROM mountain WHERE Mountain_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Mountain_ID FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the average prominence of all mountains that have been climbed.

sql: WITH ClimberCounts AS (SELECT Mountain_ID, COUNT(*) AS NumClimbers FROM climber GROUP BY Mountain_ID) SELECT T1.Name, T2.NumClimbers FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN ClimberCounts AS T2 ON T1.Mountain_ID = T2.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mountain by name, along with the total number of climbers who have climbed it.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM mountain WHERE Country NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM climber);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique countries that contain mountains but have no recorded climbers in the database.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM climber ORDER BY Points DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and country of the climber with the second-highest point total.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Height > 5150 UNION SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Range = 'Kilimanjaro';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and height of mountains that are either taller than 5150 meters or are part of the 'Kilimanjaro' range.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, each containing the name of a scientist.

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours = 156;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and hours for the project that has a duration of exactly 156 hours.

sql: SELECT Scientist FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project = 'AeH1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the identifier of the scientist assigned to the project with code 'AeH1'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Scientists WHERE Name = 'Carol Smith';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the SSN and name for the scientist named 'Carol Smith'.

sql: SELECT Code, Hours FROM Projects ORDER BY Hours DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each project's code and the hours spent, ordered from the project with the most hours to the least.

sql: SELECT * FROM Projects WHERE Name = 'Aerodynamics and Gas Mileage';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the project code and total hours for the specific project named 'Aerodynamics and Gas Mileage'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Scientist FROM AssignedTo;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, providing a distinct list of all scientists who have been assigned to a project.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN = 152934485;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the scientist identified by the specific Social Security Number 152934485.

sql: SELECT * FROM Projects WHERE Hours > 200;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all details (Code, Name, and Hours) for projects that required more than 200 hours to complete.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Scientists;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of scientists.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Project FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist's name alongside the project they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Hours FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows containing the name and total hours for every project that has an assignment.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, each pairing a scientist's name with the name of a project they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Project) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist's name along with the total number of projects they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT T2.Project, T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T1.SSN = 152934485;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the project and the name of the specific scientist identified by the SSN 152934485.

sql: SELECT T2.Scientist, SUM(T1.Hours) FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project GROUP BY T2.Scientist;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist along with the total sum of hours they have worked across all their assigned projects.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH3';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of scientists who are assigned to the 'AeH3' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Hours FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code ORDER BY T3.Hours ASC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist's name and the hours for their assigned project, ordered from the project with the lowest hours to the highest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project WHERE T2.Scientist = 222364883;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the project assigned to the scientist with ID 222364883.

sql: SELECT Project, COUNT(Scientist) FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Project;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each project along with the total count of scientists assigned to it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Project) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of all scientists who are assigned to at least one project.

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours = (SELECT MAX(Hours) FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the project that required the most hours to complete, showing the project's name and its total hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T3.Hours > (SELECT AVG(Hours) FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of scientists who are assigned to projects that require an above-average number of hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Hours) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T3.Hours) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each scientist who has worked a total of more than 150 hours across all their projects, along with their name and the corresponding total hours.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN IN (SELECT Scientist FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project = 'AeH1');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the scientist assigned to the project with the code 'AeH1'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Code NOT IN (SELECT Project FROM AssignedTo);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all projects that are currently unassigned.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo AS T2 WHERE T1.SSN = T2.Scientist);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of all scientists who are assigned to at least one project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo AS T2 JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T2.Scientist = T1.SSN AND T3.Hours < 200);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of scientists who are assigned to at least one project that requires fewer than 200 hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH1' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH3';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all unique scientists who are assigned to either project 'AeH1' or project 'AeH3'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH1' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 WHERE T1.SSN = 152934485;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a scientist assigned to project 'AeH1', after excluding the scientist with the specific SSN '152934485'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Code IN (SELECT Project FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Project HAVING COUNT(Scientist) = 1);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of projects that have exactly one scientist assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Project_Count FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Scientist, COUNT(Project) AS Project_Count FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Scientist) AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist's name along with the total number of projects they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN = (SELECT Scientist FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project = 'Ast3');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the scientist assigned to the 'Ast3' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project IN (SELECT Code FROM Projects WHERE Hours < 200);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of scientists assigned to projects that require fewer than 200 hours to complete.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH1' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project <> 'AeH3';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a scientist who is assigned to project 'AeH1' and is also assigned to at least one project other than 'AeH3'.

sql: SELECT Code, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours > (SELECT MIN(Hours) FROM Projects) AND Hours < (SELECT MAX(Hours) FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the project code and hours for all projects, excluding those with the absolute minimum and maximum recorded hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T3.Hours) < (SELECT AVG(Hours) * 2 FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of scientists whose total hours worked across all projects is less than double the average hours of a single project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo AS T2 JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T1.SSN = T2.Scientist AND T3.Name = 'Aerodynamics and Bridge Design');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of scientists who are not assigned to the 'Aerodynamics and Bridge Design' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Project FROM AssignedTo AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.Project = T2.Code WHERE T2.Hours > (SELECT AVG(Hours) FROM Projects);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the project codes for all assignments associated with projects that required more than the average number of hours to complete.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name, T3.Hours FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T3.Hours = (SELECT MIN(T4.Hours) FROM Projects AS T4 JOIN AssignedTo AS T5 ON T4.Code = T5.Project);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the scientist, project name, and hours for the assigned project that requires the minimum amount of time.

sql: SELECT * FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows providing a complete list of all captains, showing their ID, name, assigned ship ID, age, ship class, and rank.

sql: SELECT * FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the complete details for every ship in the database, including their ID, name, type, build year, class, and flag.

sql: SELECT Name, Rank FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing a complete list of all captains and their corresponding ranks.

sql: SELECT Name, Built_Year FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and construction year for each ship in the database.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of all captains who hold the rank of 'Midshipman'.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and type of all ships registered under the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS INTEGER) > 40;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of captains who are older than 40.

sql: SELECT Name, Class FROM ship WHERE Built_Year = 1997.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and class for all ships that were built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain ORDER BY CAST(age AS INTEGER) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and age of each captain, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT Name, Flag FROM ship ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each ship's name and its flag (country of registration), ordered alphabetically by the ship's name.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each captain's name alongside the name of the ship they command.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each distinct captain rank along with the total number of captains who hold that rank.

sql: SELECT Flag, COUNT(*) FROM ship GROUP BY Flag;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship's flag of registration along with the total count of ships for each flag.

sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all captains.

sql: SELECT MIN(Built_Year), MAX(Built_Year) FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the earliest (oldest) and latest (newest) build years across all ships in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = 1997.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of captains who command ships that were built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT Built_Year, COUNT(*) FROM ship GROUP BY Built_Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each year ships were built, along with a count of the total number of ships constructed in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'Panamax';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of captains who command ships of the 'Panamax' type.

sql: SELECT Rank, AVG(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each captain rank along with the average age for captains holding that rank.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of ships registered under the 'Panama' flag along with the name of each ship's captain.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID = (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Name = 'HMS Manxman');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the captain for the ship named 'HMS Manxman'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM ship WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all ships that have an assigned captain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ship AS T2 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of captains who are assigned to an existing ship.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the captain ranks held by more than one person, along with the total count of captains for each of those ranks.

sql: SELECT Built_Year, COUNT(*) FROM ship GROUP BY Built_Year HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each year in which more than one ship was built, along with the total count of ships constructed for that year.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.age) FROM (SELECT CAST(c.age as REAL) as age FROM captain AS c JOIN ship AS s ON c.Ship_ID = s.Ship_ID WHERE s.Type = 'Panamax') as T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the average age of captains who command 'Panamax' type ships.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS INTEGER) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name and age of captains who are older than the average age of all captains.

sql: SELECT Name, Built_Year FROM ship WHERE Built_Year < (SELECT AVG(Built_Year) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and build year for each ship that was constructed before the average build year of the entire fleet.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = (SELECT MIN(Built_Year) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of each captain commanding the fleet's oldest ship(s).

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE CAST(T1.age AS INTEGER) = (SELECT MIN(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of ships that are commanded by the youngest captain(s).

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain UNION SELECT Name FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows containing a consolidated, unique list of all names found in either the captain or the ship table.

sql: SELECT Name FROM ship WHERE Ship_ID NOT IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain WHERE Name = 'Captain Sir Henry Langford');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of ships that are not captained by 'Captain Sir Henry Langford'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = 1997.0 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of captains whose ships were both built in the year 1997 and are flagged under 'Panama'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = 1997.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains who command Panamanian-flagged ships, excluding any whose ships were built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT T2.Flag, AVG(CAST(T1.age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Flag;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each ship's flag and the corresponding average age of its captains.

sql: SELECT T2.Type FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Type HAVING COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the type of ship that has been commanded by two or more captains.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman' AND Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Class = 'KR');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains with the rank of 'Midshipman' who are assigned to 'KR' class ships.

sql: SELECT Name, age, CASE WHEN CAST(age AS INTEGER) < 45 THEN 'Mid-age' WHEN CAST(age AS INTEGER) >= 45 THEN 'Senior' END AS Age_Category FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each captain's name and age, along with a calculated 'Age_Category' classifying them as 'Mid-age' (under 45) or 'Senior' (45 and older).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = (SELECT MAX(Built_Year) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of the captains and the names of their ships for all ships built in the most recent year.

sql: SELECT Name, Rank FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS INTEGER) = (SELECT MAX(CAST(age AS INTEGER)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and rank of the oldest captain in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail ORDER BY Num_of_Audience DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing festival names and their audience counts, ordered from the most attended festival to the least.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and type of all artworks that are classified as a 'Program Music/Variety Show'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Result FROM nomination;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique possible outcomes for a nomination, such as 'Nominated' or 'Won'.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Location FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of all festivals that took place in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT Chair_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Panasonic Awards';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the chairperson for the 'Panasonic Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T2.Result FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the nomination results, such as 'Won' or 'Nominated', for the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all artworks, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > 155;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and audience counts for well-attended festivals, specifically those with more than 155 attendees.

sql: SELECT * FROM artwork WHERE Name = 'Indonesian Idol';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for the artwork specifically named 'Indonesian Idol'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each nominated artwork and its corresponding nomination result.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival's name along with the total count of artworks nominated for that festival.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Festival_ID) FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each artwork by name along with the total count of its festival nominations.

sql: SELECT Year, AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each festival year alongside the average number of audience members for that year.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, T2.Chair_Name FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the festival name and chairperson for each festival that received more than one artwork nomination.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of festivals that have had at least one winning nomination.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of artworks that have not won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T2.Name, T3.Result FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T3 ON T1.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T3.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the festival name, the nominated artwork's name, and the nomination result for all festivals located in the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type of each artwork that has won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Year, T1.Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the festival name, year, and number of audience members for festivals that had a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(*) FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artwork type along with the total number of nominations that type has received.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of festivals with an above-average audience size, along with their specific audience count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007 AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and type of the artwork that won an award at a festival in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Chair_Name, COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' GROUP BY T1.Chair_Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each festival chair's name alongside the total count of artworks that won an award at their respective festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Year FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Year ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the year that had the highest number of artwork nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination GROUP BY Artwork_ID HAVING COUNT(Artwork_ID) > 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T2 WHERE T2.Festival_ID = T1.Festival_ID AND T2.Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of festivals that have never had a winning nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID ORDER BY COUNT(Artwork_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all artworks that were nominated for the festival with the highest number of nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for an award but ultimately did not win.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = 1 UNION SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and type of unique artworks that were nominated for either festival ID 1 or festival ID 2.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Artwork_ID) as c FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID) AS T);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nomination AS T2 WHERE T2.Artwork_ID = T1.Artwork_ID AND T2.Result = 'Won') > 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type for all artworks that have won at least one nomination.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won') AND T1.Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Nominated');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of festivals which have had both winning entries and entries that were only nominated.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007 GROUP BY T1.Name, T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names and types of artworks that received a nomination for a festival in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(*) as Win_Count FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' GROUP BY T1.Type ORDER BY Win_Count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each artwork type and its total number of wins, ordered from the type with the most wins to the least.

sql: WITH FestivalNominations AS (SELECT Festival_ID, COUNT(Artwork_ID) as NomCount FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID) SELECT T2.Festival_Name, T2.Year, FN.NomCount FROM FestivalNominations AS FN JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON FN.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE FN.NomCount > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing festivals that received more than one nomination, showing each festival's name, the year it was held, and its total count of nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Year, T1.Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID JOIN artwork AS T3 ON T2.Artwork_ID = T3.Artwork_ID WHERE T3.Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show' AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the festival name, year, and audience count for festivals where a 'Program Music/Variety Show' won an award.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T2 JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Artwork_ID = T1.Artwork_ID AND T3.Chair_Name = 'Charles Coody');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for a festival chaired by 'Charles Coody'.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T2.Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T3 ON T1.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T3.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T3.Result = 'Nominated' AND T1.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Chair_Name = 'Raymond Floyd');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the year and name of an artwork that was nominated at the festival chaired by 'Raymond Floyd'.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Location FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Artwork_ID = (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM artwork WHERE Name = 'I Know'));
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and location of the festival where the artwork 'I Know' was nominated.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Festival_Name, COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) AS Nomination_Count FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Year, T1.Festival_Name ORDER BY T1.Year, Nomination_Count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival's name, year, and its total number of artwork nominations, ordered chronologically by year and then by the highest nomination count for that year.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT AllergyType FROM Allergy_Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique allergy type available in the system.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 20;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than 20.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students from the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Major = 600;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the first and last names of all students belonging to the major identified by the code 600.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing the first and last names of all female students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each unique allergy recorded in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name for the student with the specific student ID 1002.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the specific allergies that are categorized as 'food'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total number of students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 58 rows that list the first name, last name, and a specific allergy for each student with a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major and the total number of students enrolled in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 28 rows listing the first and last names of all students identified as having a food allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.Allergy) AS AllergyCount FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 29 rows listing each student's first name and last name, along with the total count of their known allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Allergy, COUNT(T2.StuID) AS StudentCount FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 LEFT JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy GROUP BY T1.Allergy;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each possible allergy type along with the total count of students who have that specific allergy.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each student major along with the average age of students in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first and last names of students who have more than one recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT AllergyType, COUNT(*) FROM Allergy_Type GROUP BY AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each allergy type and the total count of recorded instances for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students who are allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'HKG' AND T1.Age > 18;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who are older than 18 and from Hong Kong (city code 'HKG').

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Nuts';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a food allergy, excluding those with a specific allergy to nuts.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student) AND StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of students who have a known allergy and are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL' GROUP BY T2.Allergy ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most frequent allergy among students from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy NOT IN (SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food');
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the first and last names of students who have allergies that are not classified as food-related.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.city_code HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the city codes for students who have been identified as having more than one allergy.

sql: SELECT T2.AllergyType, AVG(T3.Age) FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy JOIN Student AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID GROUP BY T2.AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each allergy type alongside the average age of students diagnosed with that specific allergy.

sql: SELECT T2.Allergy, COUNT(T1.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age < 20 GROUP BY T2.Allergy;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each allergy type along with the total count of students under the age of 20 who have that allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T2.Fname FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.city_code != T2.city_code WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID AND T1.Major = 600;
result_summary: Returns 144 rows listing unique pairs of first names for students who share the same major (ID 600) but live in different cities.

sql: SELECT T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(T3.Age) FROM Student AS T3 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the allergy of the oldest student who has a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Advisor > 7000 AND T2.Allergy NOT IN (SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food');
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of students who are assigned to an advisor with an ID greater than 7000 and have a recorded allergy that is not food-related.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY COUNT(T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major with the highest number of students who have allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T2.StuID, T1.Allergy FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy AND T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 184 rows, identifying every unique pair of students who share a common allergy by listing their respective student IDs and the specific allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Major, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.AllergyType) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy GROUP BY T1.Major;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each student major alongside a count of the distinct allergy types found among students in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Eggs' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Milk';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who are allergic to either 'Eggs' or 'Milk'.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Allergy) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city code with the highest number of unique student allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food' AND T1.LName != 'Smith';
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a food allergy, excluding any students with the last name 'Smith'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food' GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Allergy) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY AVG(T1.Age) ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major that has the youngest average age among students with a recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'WAS' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Tree Pollen';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last names of all students who either live in the city with code 'WAS' or have a 'Tree Pollen' allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Major = (SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE StuID = 1001) AND T1.StuID != 1001;
result_summary: Returns 37 rows listing the first and last names of students who have an allergy and share the same major as the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing all available details for customers who are classified as 'good'.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name and daily hire cost for all products classified as 'Cutlery'.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id, amount_payable FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows for all bookings with a 'Confirmed' status, showing the booking ID, the associated customer ID, and the amount payable for each.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of customers.

sql: SELECT * FROM Discount_Coupons ORDER BY date_issued ASC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the earliest 5 discount coupons issued, ordered chronologically from oldest to newest.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_description FROM Products_for_Hire ORDER BY product_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and description of each product available for hire, ordered alphabetically by product name.

sql: SELECT * FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing all payment details for transactions that were made with 'Cash'.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE returned_damaged_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the booking and customer IDs for all transactions where an item was returned damaged.

sql: SELECT avg(coupon_amount) FROM Discount_Coupons;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average amount for all discount coupons.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT first_name FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the unique first names of all customers.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, count(T2.booking_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's first and last name along with the total count of bookings they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, sum(T2.amount_paid) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each customer's ID and first name along with the total sum of all payments they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, sum(T2.booked_count) FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id GROUP BY T1.booking_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each booking ID along with the total quantity of products booked within that booking.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.booking_status_code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name and last name of each customer alongside the status code for each of their bookings.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name for customers who have made a payment using a credit card.

sql: SELECT booking_status_code, sum(amount_payable) FROM Bookings GROUP BY booking_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each booking status code and the corresponding total payable amount for all bookings with that status.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of all products for hire that have been booked at least once.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.coupon_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id WHERE T2.coupon_amount > 500;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first name, last name, and coupon amount for customers who possess a high-value discount coupon worth more than $500.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, T3.product_name FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each booking ID alongside the name of the specific product included in that booking.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(T2.booking_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the customer ID, first name, and last name for all customers who have made more than one booking.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Payments AS T5 ON T2.booking_id = T5.booking_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'Book collection A' AND T5.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID, first name, and last name of the unique customer who booked 'Book collection A' and paid for it using a credit card.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE (SELECT SUM(T2.amount_paid) FROM Payments AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id) < (SELECT SUM(T3.amount_payable) FROM Bookings AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer ID and first name for any customer whose total payments made are less than their total amount payable from bookings.

sql: SELECT product_id, product_name FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM Products_Booked WHERE returned_late_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and name of all hireable products that have never been returned late.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, SUM(T2.amount_of_discount) AS total_discount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_discount DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 customers with the highest total accumulated discounts from their bookings, ordered from the largest discount to the smallest.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T2.booking_status_code = 'Provisional') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Bookings AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T3.booking_status_code != 'Provisional');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the customer ID and first name for 'good' customers who have only ever made 'Provisional' bookings.

sql: SELECT avg(product_count) FROM (SELECT T1.booking_id, count(T2.product_id) AS product_count FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id GROUP BY T1.booking_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average number of products per booking.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id WHERE T2.coupon_amount > 600;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing the first and last names of customers who are either flagged as 'good' or have a discount coupon valued over 600.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id WHERE T2.coupon_amount > 500;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the customer IDs for customers who are both classified as 'good' and have also used a high-value discount coupon worth more than 500.

sql: SELECT customer_id FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' EXCEPT SELECT customer_id FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the customer IDs for all customers classified as 'good' who have never made a payment by check.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.returned_damaged_yn = '1' AND T3.daily_hire_cost > 20;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the booking IDs for transactions where an item with a daily hire cost over 20 was returned damaged.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, sum(T1.booked_count) AS total_booked FROM Products_Booked AS T1 JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name ORDER BY total_booked DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the most frequently booked product and the total number of times it has been booked.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' AND amount_outstanding > 0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the customer ID, first name, and last name for all customers who have a confirmed booking with an outstanding balance.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, sum(T3.amount_paid) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Payments AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T2.booking_start_date LIKE '2017-%' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows summarizing the total amount each customer paid for bookings made in 2017, listing the customer's ID and first name.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN View_Product_Availability AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.booking_id = 5 AND T2.available_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the product that is available for hire for the booking with an ID of 5.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING sum(T2.amount_outstanding) > sum(T2.amount_of_refund);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, (SELECT max(T2.booking_end_date) FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id) AS last_booking_date FROM Customers AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's ID and first name, along with the date of their most recent booking.

sql: SELECT T4.product_type_code, sum(T3.booked_amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' GROUP BY T4.product_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product type code along with the total booked amount for that type, calculated only from bookings made by 'good' customers.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T3.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name of customers who have booked more than one distinct product across all their bookings.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name FROM Products_Booked AS T1 JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING sum(CASE WHEN T1.returned_late_yn = '1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > sum(CASE WHEN T1.returned_late_yn = '0' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the product that is more frequently returned late than on time.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, sum(T2.amount_payable) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.date_became_customer LIKE '2017-%' GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING sum(T2.amount_payable) > 200;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for customers who joined in 2017 and have a total payable amount over 200, showing their ID, first name, and the sum of their booking amounts.

sql: SELECT * FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing the complete profile for every person, including their ID, sex, name, date of birth, height, and weight.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all available candidate polling data, including the poll source, date, and the candidate's support, consideration, opposition, and unsure rates.

sql: SELECT Name, Date_of_Birth FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing the names and dates of birth for all individuals identified as male.

sql: SELECT Candidate_ID, Support_rate FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the candidate ID and their corresponding support rate, filtered specifically for results from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height = 188;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a person whose height is exactly 188.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate = 0.25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all polling details for a candidate with a specific support rate of exactly 25%.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing people's names and their heights, ordered from tallest to shortest.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value which is the total count of all people.

sql: SELECT avg(Support_rate) FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average support rate calculated across all candidates.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Poll_Source FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique poll source from the candidate data.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's name and their associated polling source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.Candidate_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each person's name and the total count of their candidacies.

sql: SELECT T2.Poll_Source, avg(T2.Support_rate) FROM candidate AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each polling source and the average candidate support rate calculated from that source's polls.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the candidate who is female.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all individuals who are identified as candidates.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height > (SELECT avg(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and height of each person who is taller than the overall average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Support_rate DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the candidate with the highest support rate.

sql: SELECT T2.Date, count(*) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Date;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each date along with the total count of candidates for that date.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Oppose_rate > T2.Support_rate;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows with the names of candidates whose opposition rate is greater than their support rate.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the names of people who are not listed as candidates.
