sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > (SELECT avg(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates whose support rate is higher than the average support rate across all candidates.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the poll sources that are associated with more than one candidate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID = (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate ORDER BY Support_rate DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, height, and weight of the candidate who has the highest support rate.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M' UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 190;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique names of all people who are either male or have a height greater than 190.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate < 0.2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a candidate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll' who has a support rate of 20% or greater.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, avg(T1.Height), avg(T1.Weight) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the average height and average weight of political candidates, grouped by sex.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source ORDER BY avg(Support_rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns the single poll source that, on average, reports the highest support rate for candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Unsure_rate > (SELECT avg(Unsure_rate) FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the candidate whose "Unsure rate" is greater than the average unsure rate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Support_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM people WHERE Sex = 'F');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and support rate for a candidate identified as female.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Date_of_Birth LIKE '%.1976';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all candidates who were born in the year 1976.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height = (SELECT max(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the tallest person who is also a candidate.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of individuals who are both male and registered as a candidate.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all males who are not registered as candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.s_rate_minus_o_rate FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT People_ID, Support_rate - Oppose_rate AS s_rate_minus_o_rate FROM candidate) AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.s_rate_minus_o_rate DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each candidate's name alongside their net support rate (support rate minus oppose rate), ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Oppose_rate > 0.4);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates who have an opposition rate greater than 40%.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1, candidate AS T2 WHERE T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID AND T2.Support_rate = (SELECT min(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and poll source for the candidate with the minimum support rate.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll') AND T1.Sex = 'M';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the male candidate who was included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Name LIKE '%e%' GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of candidates whose names contain the letter 'e'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > 188 AND T1.Weight > 83;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates who are taller than 188cm and weigh more than 83kg.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > 0.2 AND T2.Oppose_rate < 0.44;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of candidates with a support rate over 20% and an opposition rate under 44%.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Artist ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 275 rows listing the name of each artist in the database, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, City FROM Customer WHERE Country = 'Brazil';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first name, last name, and city for all customers located in Brazil.

sql: SELECT Name, Milliseconds FROM Track ORDER BY Milliseconds DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and millisecond durations for the 5 longest tracks, ordered from longest to shortest.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM Album AS T1 JOIN Artist AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId WHERE T2.Name = 'Accept';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of all albums by the artist 'Accept'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Customer;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all customers.

sql: SELECT InvoiceId, BillingAddress, Total FROM Invoice WHERE Total > 10.00;
result_summary: Returns 64 rows listing invoices with a total sale amount greater than $10.00, showing each invoice's ID, billing address, and total value.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName, Title FROM Employee;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the last name, first name, and job title for every employee in the company.

sql: SELECT Name, Composer FROM Track WHERE UnitPrice = 0.99;
result_summary: Returns 3290 rows listing the name and composer for all music tracks with a unit price of $0.99.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Genre;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the names of all available music genres in the database.

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, Email FROM Employee WHERE Title = 'General Manager';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name, last name, and email for the employee with the title 'General Manager'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, COUNT(T2.TrackId) FROM Album AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.AlbumId = T2.AlbumId GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 347 rows, providing a count of the number of tracks for each album title.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows listing each customer's first and last name alongside their total spending, calculated by summing all of their invoice totals.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Track AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId JOIN Playlist AS T3 ON T2.PlaylistId = T3.PlaylistId WHERE T3.Name = 'Music';
result_summary: Returns 6580 rows listing the names of all tracks that belong to the playlist named 'Music'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.TrackId) FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing each music genre name alongside the total count of tracks available for that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.SupportRepId = T2.EmployeeId WHERE T2.FirstName = 'Jane' AND T2.LastName = 'Peacock';
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who are assigned to the support representative named Jane Peacock.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId GROUP BY T1.ArtistId HAVING COUNT(T2.AlbumId) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of artists who have released more than 5 albums.

sql: SELECT BillingCountry, SUM(Total) FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry ORDER BY SUM(Total) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the total invoice amount for each billing country, ordered from the country with the highest total sales to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Track AS T1 JOIN MediaType AS T2 ON T1.MediaTypeId = T2.MediaTypeId WHERE T2.Name = 'MPEG audio file';
result_summary: Returns 3034 rows listing the names of all tracks that are in the 'MPEG audio file' media format.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.ReportsTo = T2.EmployeeId;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each employee's first and last name alongside the first and last name of their direct manager.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Quantity) FROM Track AS T1 JOIN InvoiceLine AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY SUM(T2.Quantity) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 1888 rows listing each track's name alongside its total sales quantity, ordered to show the most-sold tracks first.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the top 5 customers by their total spending, showing each customer's first name, last name, and total purchase amount, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: WITH CountryGenreSales AS (SELECT I.BillingCountry, G.Name AS GenreName, SUM(IL.UnitPrice * IL.Quantity) AS TotalSales FROM Invoice AS I JOIN InvoiceLine AS IL ON I.InvoiceId = IL.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T ON IL.TrackId = T.TrackId JOIN Genre AS G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId GROUP BY I.BillingCountry, G.Name) SELECT T1.BillingCountry, T1.GenreName, T1.TotalSales FROM CountryGenreSales AS T1 WHERE T1.TotalSales = (SELECT MAX(T2.TotalSales) FROM CountryGenreSales AS T2 WHERE T2.BillingCountry = T1.BillingCountry) ORDER BY T1.BillingCountry;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows identifying the top-selling music genre(s) for each country, along with the corresponding total sales, ordered alphabetically by country.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Genre AS T5 ON T4.GenreId = T5.GenreId WHERE T1.CustomerId IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T1.CustomerId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T5.GenreId) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have purchased music from more than one distinct genre.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T3.Total) AS TotalSales FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeId = T2.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice AS T3 ON T2.CustomerId = T3.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.EmployeeId ORDER BY TotalSales DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each support representative's name and their total sales generated from assigned customers, ordered from highest to lowest sales.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Track WHERE TrackId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine);
result_summary: Returns 1519 rows listing the names of all tracks that have never been purchased or appeared on an invoice.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T3.TrackId) AS RockTrackCount FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T3.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T4.Name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T1.ArtistId ORDER BY RockTrackCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single artist with the most rock tracks in the catalog, showing their name and the total count.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId WHERE T1.Country = 'USA' GROUP BY T1.CustomerId HAVING SUM(T2.Total) > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM Invoice);
result_summary: Returns 13 rows identifying US customers whose total spending is greater than the overall average invoice value, showing each customer's name and their total amount spent.

sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS REAL) * 100 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track) FROM Track AS T1 JOIN MediaType AS T2 ON T1.MediaTypeId = T2.MediaTypeId WHERE T2.Name = 'MPEG audio file';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the calculated percentage of all tracks that are of the 'MPEG audio file' media type.

sql: SELECT T1.CustomerId FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Album AS T5 ON T4.AlbumId = T5.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T6 ON T5.ArtistId = T6.ArtistId WHERE T6.Name = 'AC/DC' EXCEPT SELECT T1.CustomerId FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Album AS T5 ON T4.AlbumId = T5.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T6 ON T5.ArtistId = T6.ArtistId WHERE T6.Name = 'Accept';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique IDs of customers who have purchased music by 'AC/DC' but have not purchased any music by 'Accept'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Milliseconds) / 3600000.0 AS TotalHours FROM Playlist AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.PlaylistId = T2.PlaylistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.TrackId = T3.TrackId GROUP BY T1.PlaylistId;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each playlist by name, along with the total duration of all its tracks calculated in hours.

sql: SELECT T1.City, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSales FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.City ORDER BY TotalSales DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 cities with the highest total sales, ordered from highest to lowest sales amount.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Track AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.TrackId = T2.TrackId GROUP BY T1.TrackId HAVING COUNT(T2.PlaylistId) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3503 rows listing the name of each track that appears on more than one playlist.

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.ReportsTo = T2.EmployeeId WHERE T1.ReportsTo IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T1.ReportsTo ORDER BY COUNT(T1.EmployeeId) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the first and last name of the manager who has the most direct reports.

sql: SELECT AVG(TrackCount) FROM (SELECT T1.InvoiceId, COUNT(T2.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Invoice AS T1 JOIN InvoiceLine AS T2 ON T1.InvoiceId = T2.InvoiceId WHERE T1.BillingCountry = 'Germany' GROUP BY T1.InvoiceId) AS T3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of tracks purchased on each invoice for transactions originating from Germany.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Album AS T5 ON T4.AlbumId = T5.AlbumId WHERE T5.Title = 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You' GROUP BY T1.CustomerId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.TrackId) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track WHERE AlbumId = 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Milliseconds FROM Track AS T1 WHERE T1.Milliseconds > (SELECT AVG(T2.Milliseconds) FROM Track AS T2 WHERE T2.GenreId = T1.GenreId);
result_summary: Returns 1539 rows listing the names and lengths of all tracks that are longer than the average length for their respective genre.

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, City FROM Customer WHERE City IN (SELECT DISTINCT City FROM Employee);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name, last name, and city of customers who are located in the same city as a company employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Bytes) AS TotalBytes FROM Playlist AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.PlaylistId = T2.PlaylistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.TrackId = T3.TrackId GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY TotalBytes DESC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the total size (in bytes) for each playlist, ordered from the largest playlist to the smallest.

sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', InvoiceDate) AS InvoiceYear, SUM(Total) AS YearlySales FROM Invoice GROUP BY InvoiceYear ORDER BY InvoiceYear;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the total sales amount for each year, ordered chronologically from the earliest to the most recent year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T3.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T4.Name IN ('Rock', 'Jazz') GROUP BY T1.ArtistId, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.Name) = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student;

sql: SELECT * FROM Pets;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all pets and providing their ID, type, age, and weight.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE LName = 'Smith';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students with the last name 'Smith'.

sql: SELECT PetID, pet_age, weight FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID, age, and weight for all pets identified as dogs.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT PetID, PetType, pet_age FROM Pets ORDER BY pet_age ASC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID, type, and age for each pet, ordered from youngest to oldest.

sql: SELECT StuID, Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'HKG';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for students from the city with the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT PetType FROM Pets;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing a unique list of all pet types.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.PetID FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows matching students to their pets, listing each student's first and last name along with the corresponding Pet ID.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T2.PetType, T2.pet_age FROM Has_Pet AS T1 JOIN Pets AS T2 ON T1.PetID = T2.PetID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each pet's type and age alongside the ID of the student who owns it.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.PetID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last name of each student along with the total number of pets they own.

sql: SELECT PetType, COUNT(PetID) FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pet type along with the total count of pets for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.PetType, T3.pet_age, T3.weight FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each student's first and last name along with the type, age, and weight for each pet they own.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student who owns a cat.

sql: SELECT T1.PetType, AVG(T1.weight) FROM Pets AS T1 GROUP BY T1.PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each distinct pet type alongside the calculated average weight for that type.

sql: SELECT T3.PetID, T3.PetType, T3.pet_age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID, type, and age of pets belonging to students who live in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, AVG(T3.pet_age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each student's first name along with the average age of all the pets they own.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name, last name, and major for any student who has a pet that is 3 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING count(T2.PetID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own more than one pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age > (SELECT AVG(pet_age) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first and last name of any student who owns a pet that is older than the average age of all pets.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Pet);
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the first and last names of all students who do not own a pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight = (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the first and last name of the student who owns the heaviest pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'dog' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last names of students who own a dog but do not own a cat.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own a cat but do not own a dog.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Sex = T2.Sex AND T1.Major = T2.Major WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 100 rows, listing all unique pairs of students who share the same gender and academic major by showing the first and last names for each student in the pair.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.PetType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.Age > 20;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT PetType FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType HAVING MAX(pet_age) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the pet type for which the oldest pet of that type is more than two years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.PetType) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who own pets of only a single, uniform type (e.g., only dogs, or only cats, but not both).

sql: SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major of the youngest student in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight > (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'cat');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a student who owns a pet that weighs more than the heaviest cat on record.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, SUM(T3.weight) AS total_pet_weight FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each student's first and last name alongside the total combined weight of all pets owned by that student.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code ORDER BY COUNT(T2.PetID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city code that has the highest number of student-owned pets.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.Sex = 'F' AND T3.weight > 10.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of female students who own a pet weighing more than 10.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = (SELECT PetType FROM Pets ORDER BY pet_age DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the student who owns a pet of the same type as the single oldest pet in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of students who are older than the average age of all students who own a pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.PetType, T3.weight FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight = (SELECT MAX(P.weight) FROM Has_Pet AS HP JOIN Pets AS P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID WHERE HP.StuID = T1.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying each student's heaviest pet, listing the student's first and last name along with that pet's type and weight.

sql: SELECT T2.PetType FROM Has_Pet AS T1 JOIN Pets AS T2 ON T1.PetID = T2.PetID GROUP BY T2.PetType ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most popular pet type, determined by which type is owned by the highest number of unique students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age < (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Student AS T4 WHERE T4.Major = T1.Major);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique first and last names of students whose pet's age is less than the total number of students in their own major.

sql: SELECT dorm_name FROM Dorm WHERE gender = 'M';

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.room_number FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid;

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;

sql: SELECT dormid, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfStudents FROM Lives_in GROUP BY dormid;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, providing a count of the number of students living in each dormitory, identified by its `dormid`.

sql: SELECT Sex, AVG(Age) AS AverageAge FROM Student GROUP BY Sex;

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid WHERE T2.stuid = 1001;

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid WHERE T2.dormid = 100;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of all students who live in the dormitory with ID 100.

sql: SELECT dormid, COUNT(amenid) AS NumberOfAmenities FROM Has_amenity GROUP BY dormid;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each dormitory ID along with the total number of amenities available for that dorm.

sql: SELECT city_code, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfStudents FROM Student GROUP BY city_code;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows, listing each city code along with the total count of students from that city.

sql: SELECT T2.amenity_name FROM Has_amenity AS T1 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T2 ON T1.amenid = T2.amenid WHERE T1.dormid = 109;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all amenities available for the specific dorm with an ID of 109.

sql: SELECT T1.stuid, T2.dorm_name FROM Lives_in AS T1 JOIN Dorm AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows listing each student's ID alongside the name of the dormitory they live in.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid WHERE T2.dormid = (SELECT dormid FROM Dorm WHERE dorm_name = 'Smith Hall');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of all students who live in the 'Smith Hall' dorm.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T1.dorm_name HAVING count(T2.stuid) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of dorms that have more than one student resident.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Has_amenity AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T1.dormid, T1.dorm_name HAVING COUNT(T2.amenid) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of dormitories that have more than two amenities.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Has_amenity AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T4 ON T3.amenid = T4.amenid WHERE T4.amenity_name = 'TV Lounge';
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the first and last names of all students who live in a dormitory that has a 'TV Lounge' amenity.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT stuid FROM Lives_in WHERE dormid IN (SELECT dormid FROM Has_amenity GROUP BY dormid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1));
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the first and last names of students who live in dormitories that have more than one amenity.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid JOIN Student AS T3 ON T2.stuid = T3.stuid WHERE T3.city_code = 'HKG';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of dormitories that house students from the city represented by the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT dorm_name, student_capacity FROM Dorm WHERE student_capacity = (SELECT MAX(student_capacity) FROM Dorm);
result_summary: Returns 1 row that identifies the dorm with the highest student capacity, listing the dorm's name and its capacity.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age > 18 UNION SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 22 rows containing the unique first and last names of students who are either older than 18 or from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT dormid FROM Lives_in WHERE stuid = 1002 INTERSECT SELECT dormid FROM Lives_in WHERE stuid = 1003;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the shared dormitory ID for the students with IDs 1002 and 1003.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid WHERE T2.dormid = (SELECT dormid FROM Lives_in WHERE stuid = 1002) AND T1.StuID <> 1002;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of all other students who live in the same dorm as the student with ID 1002.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Has_amenity AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T3 ON T2.amenid = T3.amenid WHERE T3.amenity_name = 'Study Room';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all dorms that have a 'Study Room' amenity.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid WHERE T3.student_capacity > 110;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the first and last names of students who live in large-capacity dorms that can house more than 110 students.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T1.dorm_name ORDER BY count(T2.stuid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the single most populated dorm, determined by counting the number of students living in each and selecting the top result.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN (SELECT dormid, COUNT(*) AS amenity_count FROM Has_amenity GROUP BY dormid) AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid WHERE T2.amenity_count > 2;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of dorms that have more than two amenities.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.student_capacity) FROM Dorm AS T1 WHERE T1.dormid IN (SELECT DISTINCT dormid FROM Has_amenity);
result_summary: Returns a single value representing the average student capacity for all dorms that have at least one recorded amenity.

sql: SELECT Major FROM Student GROUP BY Major HAVING COUNT(StuID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid JOIN Student AS T3 ON T2.stuid = T3.stuid GROUP BY T1.dorm_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Sex) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of dorms that house students of more than one gender.

sql: SELECT T3.amenity_name FROM Has_amenity AS T2 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T3 ON T2.amenid = T3.amenid WHERE T2.dormid = (SELECT T1.dormid FROM Lives_in AS T1 JOIN Student AS T4 ON T1.stuid = T4.stuid WHERE T4.Fname = 'Linda' AND T4.LName = 'Smith');

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Journal_ID FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T2.Editor_ID FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the theme of each journal and the corresponding ID of an editor assigned to its committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Theme, T3.Work_Type FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T3 ON T1.Editor_ID = T3.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T2 ON T3.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each editor's name, the theme of the journal they work on, and their specific work type for that journal's committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each editor's name and the total number of journal committees they serve on.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, COUNT(T2.Editor_ID) FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each journal theme along with the total count of editors assigned to journals within that theme.

sql: SELECT T2.Work_Type, AVG(T1.Sales) FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T2.Work_Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the average sales calculated for each distinct type of journal work.

sql: SELECT T2.Work_Type, AVG(T1.Age) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T2.Work_Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each work type from the journal committee along with the average age of the editors assigned to that type of work.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo';

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID JOIN editor AS T3 ON T2.Editor_ID = T3.Editor_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Kamila Porczyk';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Journal_ID = 13;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM editor AS T1 WHERE T1.Editor_ID IN (SELECT Editor_ID FROM journal_committee WHERE Work_Type = 'Photo');

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any editor who serves on the committee for more than one journal.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Journal_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Editor_ID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY count(T2.Journal_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme ORDER BY count(T2.Editor_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM editor WHERE Age > (SELECT Age FROM editor WHERE Name = 'Marek Siudym');

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.distance) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T2.name = 'Boeing 747-400';

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid GROUP BY T1.eid HAVING count(T2.aid) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 737-800';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.eid = T3.eid WHERE T1.origin = 'Los Angeles';

sql: SELECT T2.name, count(T1.eid) FROM certificate AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid GROUP BY T2.name HAVING count(T1.eid) >= 1;

sql: SELECT T2.flno, T1.name, T1.distance AS max_range, T2.distance AS flight_distance FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T2.distance < T1.distance / 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying flights that operated at less than half their aircraft's maximum range, showing the flight number, aircraft name, its maximum range, and the actual distance flown.

result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the employee who holds the most certificates.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM aircraft AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.aid = T1.aid);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all aircraft that have not been used in any recorded flights.

result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing the employee IDs (`eid`) for individuals who have been awarded both certificate #2 and certificate #10.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T1.origin, T1.price FROM flight AS T1 WHERE T1.price > (SELECT AVG(T2.price) FROM flight AS T2 WHERE T2.origin = T1.origin);

result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all flight details for flights that use an aircraft the employee 'John Williams' is certified to operate.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employee WHERE salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employee);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the highest-paid employee, showing their name and salary.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T1.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee) GROUP BY T1.eid;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and salary of employees who hold a certificate and earn more than the company-wide average salary.

sql: SELECT flno, distance FROM flight WHERE distance < (SELECT AVG(distance) FROM aircraft);

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.medicine_id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.enzyme_id) FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each medicine by name and the total count of enzymes it interacts with.

sql: SELECT Location, COUNT(id) FROM enzyme GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct cellular location and the total number of enzymes found within that location.

sql: SELECT FDA_approved, COUNT(id) FROM medicine GROUP BY FDA_approved;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique names of medicines that are classified as having an 'inhibitor' interaction type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T3.FDA_approved = 'Yes';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Location = 'Mitochondrion';

sql: SELECT interaction_type, COUNT(*) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction GROUP BY interaction_type;

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING count(T2.medicine_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Trade_Name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' AND T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';

sql: SELECT name FROM enzyme WHERE Location = 'Mitochondrion' UNION SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows providing a combined list of names for all enzymes located in the 'Mitochondrion' and all medicines that are not FDA-approved.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.enzyme_id = 1 INTERSECT SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'Yes';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor' EXCEPT SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'Yes';

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id) AS interaction_count FROM enzyme AS T1;

sql: SELECT T1.Chromosome, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T2.medicine_id = T3.id GROUP BY T1.Chromosome;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each enzyme's chromosome location and the total count of distinct medicines that interact with enzymes from that specific location.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING count(T2.medicine_id) > (SELECT count(T3.medicine_id) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 WHERE T3.enzyme_id = 3);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the names of enzymes that interact with more medicines than the specific enzyme with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.enzyme_id = T2.enzyme_id AND T1.medicine_id < T2.medicine_id JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T1.medicine_id = T3.id JOIN medicine AS T4 ON T2.medicine_id = T4.id;

sql: SELECT Name, membership_amount FROM branch WHERE membership_amount > 100;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Register_Year FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN branch AS T3 ON T2.Branch_ID = T3.Branch_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Alexandre';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Member_ID) FROM branch AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN membership_register_branch AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Register_Year > '2016';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_pounds) FROM branch AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Branch_ID = T2.Branch_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_pounds) FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Louisville, Kentucky' AND T1.Level = 6 GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Branch_ID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN purchase AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Total_pounds) > (SELECT AVG(Total_pounds) FROM purchase);

sql: SELECT Register_Year FROM membership_register_branch GROUP BY Register_Year ORDER BY COUNT(Member_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE origin = 'Trivandrum';

sql: SELECT * FROM weekly_weather WHERE day_of_week = 'Monday';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the complete weather report from all stations for observations recorded on a Monday.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT interval FROM train;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique train schedule interval present in the dataset.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total number of stations.

sql: SELECT name FROM train ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of all trains, ordered alphabetically from A to Z.

sql: SELECT MAX(wind_speed_mph) FROM weekly_weather;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the overall maximum wind speed (in mph) recorded across all weekly weather data.

sql: SELECT * FROM train WHERE train_number = 16127;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete details for the specific train with number 16127.

sql: SELECT network_name, local_authority FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each station's network name and its corresponding local authority.

sql: SELECT T2.network_name FROM route AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.train_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the network names of all stations on the route for the train with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T3.network_name = 'Amersham';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all trains that service stations on the 'Amersham' network.

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(T2.station_id) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train by name along with the total number of stations on its route.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, AVG(T2.high_temperature), AVG(T2.low_temperature) FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each weather station network along with its calculated average weekly high and low temperatures across all associated stations.

sql: SELECT network_name FROM station WHERE id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE high_temperature > 60);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the network name of a station that has recorded a weekly high temperature greater than 60 degrees.

sql: SELECT name, origin, destination FROM train WHERE origin = 'Guruvayur' OR destination = 'Guruvayur';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, origin, and destination for all trains that either start from or end at Guruvayur.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.day_of_week, T2.precipitation FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.precipitation = (SELECT MAX(precipitation) FROM weekly_weather WHERE station_id = T1.id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing for each station network the day of the week that experienced the highest precipitation and the corresponding precipitation amount.

sql: SELECT T3.local_authority, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id GROUP BY T3.local_authority;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each local authority and the total number of distinct trains that service stations within that authority.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.wind_speed_mph > 13 AND T2.day_of_week = 'Wednesday';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of station networks that experienced high wind speeds (over 13 mph) on a Wednesday.

sql: SELECT name FROM train WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT train_id FROM route WHERE station_id = 3);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of trains that do not pass through the station with ID 3.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.services = 'Metropolitan line and Chiltern Railways' AND T2.low_temperature = 52;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the network names for stations that service the 'Metropolitan line and Chiltern Railways' and experienced a weekly low temperature of 52 degrees.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS R1 ON T1.id = R1.train_id JOIN route AS R2 ON R1.station_id = R2.station_id JOIN train AS T2 ON T2.id = R2.train_id WHERE T1.id < T2.id GROUP BY T1.name, T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing unique pairs of train names that share at least one common station on their routes.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.train_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Ananthapuri Express' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM weekly_weather AS T4 WHERE T4.station_id = T1.id AND T4.precipitation > 80);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the network names of stations on the 'Ananthapuri Express' route which have also experienced high precipitation (over 80).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T3.local_authority = 'Chiltern' GROUP BY T1.name HAVING count(T3.id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of trains that make at least one stop in a station located within the 'Chiltern' local authority.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name ORDER BY AVG(T2.wind_speed_mph) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the station network with the highest average weekly wind speed.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.interval = 'Daily' AND T3.services LIKE '%London%';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of trains that run daily and service a station in London.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.services = 'Greater Anglia' AND T1.id NOT IN (SELECT T2.station_id FROM train AS T3 JOIN route AS T2 ON T3.id = T2.train_id WHERE T3.name = 'Guruvayur Express');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the network name of a 'Greater Anglia' station that is not served by the 'Guruvayur Express' train.

sql: SELECT day_of_week FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY day_of_week ORDER BY AVG(high_temperature - low_temperature) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the day of the week with the highest average daily temperature fluctuation, calculated as the difference between the high and low temperatures.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.origin = 'Chennai' AND T3.local_authority = 'Watford';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique name of a train that originates in 'Chennai' and also has a stop at a station within the 'Watford' local authority.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.high_temperature > 65 INTERSECT SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.low_temperature < 53;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the station network name that experienced both a weekly high temperature over 65 degrees and a weekly low temperature under 53 degrees.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.network_name, MIN(T4.low_temperature) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.station_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, with each row listing a train's name, an associated station network, and the minimum weekly low temperature recorded at any station along that train's route.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id HAVING AVG(precipitation) > 30);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of trains that pass through stations with an average weekly precipitation greater than 30.

sql: SELECT T3.network_name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.origin = 'Trivandrum' EXCEPT SELECT T3.network_name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.origin = 'Chennai';

sql: SELECT T2.local_authority FROM route AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.local_authority ORDER BY COUNT(T1.train_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single local authority with the most train routes passing through its stations, determined by counting the number of trains per authority.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.destination = 'Chennai' AND T3.services LIKE '%London Overground%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique name of a train destined for 'Chennai' that also passes through a station with 'London Overground' services.

sql: SELECT T3.local_authority, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id), AVG(T4.high_temperature) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id JOIN weekly_weather AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.station_id GROUP BY T3.local_authority;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each local authority, the number of unique trains that pass through its stations, and the average weekly high temperature at those stations.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN (SELECT station_id FROM route GROUP BY station_id HAVING COUNT(train_id) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY station_id HAVING AVG(wind_speed_mph) > 10);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the network names of stations that serve more than one train route and have an average weekly wind speed greater than 10 mph.

sql: SELECT T1.day_of_week, T4.name FROM (SELECT day_of_week, MAX(high_temperature) AS max_temp FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY day_of_week) AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.day_of_week = T2.day_of_week AND T1.max_temp = T2.high_temperature JOIN route AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.station_id JOIN train AS T4 ON T3.train_id = T4.id;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the names of trains that pass through the station with the highest recorded temperature for each day of the week, showing the corresponding day and train name.

sql: SELECT T3.network_name, T4.network_name FROM route AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.train_id = T2.train_id AND T1.station_id < T2.station_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T1.station_id = T3.id JOIN station AS T4 ON T2.station_id = T4.id WHERE T3.local_authority <> T4.local_authority;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing pairs of station names that are served by the same train route but are located in different local authorities.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.day_of_week) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the station network names that have weather data recorded on more than two different days of the week.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.origin, T1.destination FROM train AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.origin = T2.destination AND T1.destination = T2.origin;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name, origin, and destination for all trains that have a corresponding return journey between the same two cities.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details, T3.service_type_code, T3.service_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T1.resident_id = 6;

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id GROUP BY T1.organization_id HAVING count(T2.service_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.resident_id, T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 WHERE T1.property_id IN (SELECT T2.property_id FROM Properties AS T2 WHERE T2.property_type_code = 'house');

sql: SELECT T1.thing_id, T1.service_details, T2.organization_details FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T1.Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Properties AS T3 ON T2.property_id = T3.property_id WHERE T3.property_type_code = 'house';

sql: SELECT T2.property_address, count(T1.resident_id) FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id GROUP BY T2.property_address;

sql: SELECT T3.organization_details, T2.service_type_code FROM Residents_Services AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id JOIN Organizations AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T1.other_details = 'Unsatisfied';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the organizations and service types associated with all cases where a resident reported being 'Unsatisfied' with a service.

sql: SELECT thing_id, Type_of_Thing_Code FROM Things WHERE Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics' UNION SELECT thing_id, Type_of_Thing_Code FROM Things WHERE Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Commen';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the ID and type code for all things that are categorized as either 'Electronics' or 'Commen'.

sql: SELECT T2.other_details, count(T1.Customer_Event_ID) FROM Customer_Events AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id GROUP BY T2.resident_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing the total number of customer events for each resident, identified by their details.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T2.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T3.Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics' AND T4.organization_details = 'Robel-Schulist Group';

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 WHERE T1.organization_id IN (SELECT T2.parent_organization_id FROM Organizations AS T2) EXCEPT SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T2.service_type_code = 'Cleanning';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details of parent organizations that do not offer a 'Cleanning' service.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id JOIN Properties AS T4 ON T2.property_id = T4.property_id WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Check' AND T4.property_type_code = 'apartment';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the unique 'other details' for residents who live in an apartment and have received a 'Check' type service.

sql: SELECT T1.property_address, count(T2.resident_id) AS resident_count FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id GROUP BY T1.property_id ORDER BY resident_count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the address and total resident count for the top 3 most populated properties, ordered from highest to lowest count.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.thing_id IN (SELECT T3.thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things AS T3 WHERE T3.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'In Progress');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique ID and details for customers who are associated with any item that currently has a status of 'In Progress'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id JOIN Residents_Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T3.resident_id IN (SELECT T4.resident_id FROM Residents AS T4 WHERE T4.date_moved_in = '2015-03-27 12:00:00');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique organization details for organizations that provide services to residents who moved in on March 27, 2015.

sql: SELECT T2.service_type_code FROM Residents_Services AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.service_id = T2.service_id GROUP BY T2.service_type_code ORDER BY count(T1.service_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the code for the most popular service type, determined by which type has been used the most times by residents.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details, count(T2.Customer_Event_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(T2.Customer_Event_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing customers who have had more than one event, along with the total count of events for each customer.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.service_id = T3.service_id WHERE T3.organization_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT T4.parent_organization_id FROM Organizations AS T4 WHERE T4.parent_organization_id IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique 'other_details' for residents who have received services from any organization that is also a parent organization.

sql: SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things WHERE Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Close' INTERSECT SELECT thing_id FROM Timed_Locations_of_Things WHERE Location_Code = '064 Charles Mou';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of a thing that has a status of 'Close' and is also located at '064 Charles Mou'.

sql: SELECT T1.property_type_code, count(*) FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.other_details = 'Unsatisfied' GROUP BY T1.property_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each property type alongside a count of all associated service reports marked as 'Unsatisfied'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Event_Notes AS T3 ON T2.Customer_Event_ID = T3.Customer_Event_ID WHERE T3.service_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique customers (ID and details) who have had a service event with a type of 'Check'.

sql: SELECT avg(thing_count) FROM (SELECT count(T1.thing_id) AS thing_count FROM Things AS T1 GROUP BY T1.organization_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the average number of things per organization.

sql: SELECT T1.other_details FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Properties AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.property_type_code = 'house' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customer_Events AS T3 WHERE T3.resident_id = T1.resident_id);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows providing details for residents who live in a 'house' and have had at least one customer event recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.service_type_code, T1.service_details FROM Services AS T1 WHERE T1.service_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.service_id FROM Residents_Services AS T2 WHERE T2.other_details = 'Unsatisfied');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the service type code and details for all services that have never had a resident report being 'Unsatisfied'.

sql: SELECT T2.customer_details, T3.Type_of_Thing_Code, T4.organization_details FROM Customer_Events AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Things AS T3 ON T1.thing_id = T3.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T4 ON T3.organization_id = T4.organization_id WHERE T1.Customer_Event_ID = 70;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the details for the customer, the type of 'thing', and the associated organization involved in the specific customer event with an ID of 70.

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details AS parent_organization, count(T3.service_id) AS total_services FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.parent_organization_id JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.organization_id = T3.organization_id WHERE T1.organization_id != T2.organization_id GROUP BY T1.organization_details;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each parent organization and the total count of services offered across all of its subsidiary organizations.

sql: SELECT T1.thing_id FROM Timed_Status_of_Things AS T1 GROUP BY T1.thing_id HAVING count(*) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs for things that have had their status recorded more than two times.

sql: SELECT T1.resident_id, T1.other_details, T2.date_provided, T1.date_moved_out FROM Residents AS T1 JOIN Residents_Services AS T2 ON T1.resident_id = T2.resident_id WHERE T2.date_provided > T1.date_moved_out;

sql: SELECT T3.Theme, AVG(T1.Age) FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID GROUP BY T3.Theme;

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID WHERE T1.Year IN ('2014', '2015') GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Year) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the stadium that hosted concerts in both 2014 and 2015.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Singer_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.concert_Name = 'Auditions';

sql: SELECT T1.Country FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the country whose singers have the most concert appearances, based on a count of performances.

sql: SELECT T2.concert_Name FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_ID = T2.concert_ID GROUP BY T1.concert_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Singer_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of concerts that featured more than one singer.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.Stadium_ID WHERE T1.Year = '2014' ORDER BY T2.Capacity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the stadium with the largest capacity that hosted a concert in 2014.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Country = 'France' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Theme = 'Bleeding Love';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM singer);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.Stadium_ID WHERE T4.Location = 'Ayr United';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of singers who have performed in a concert at the 'Ayr United' location.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Theme FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'United States';

sql: SELECT Name FROM stadium WHERE Capacity > (SELECT AVG(Capacity) FROM stadium) AND Stadium_ID IN (SELECT Stadium_ID FROM concert);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.concert_Name = 'Super bootcamp';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and country of each singer who performed at the 'Super bootcamp' concert.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID WHERE T3.Year = '2014' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a singer who is from the United States and also performed in a concert during the year 2014.

sql: SELECT Year FROM concert GROUP BY Year ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single year that had the highest number of concerts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_ID = T3.concert_ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.Stadium_ID WHERE T4.Name = 'Stark''s Park' ORDER BY T1.Age ASC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.lesson_status_code = 'Cancelled';

sql: SELECT T2.vehicle_details, SUM(T1.price) FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id GROUP BY T2.vehicle_details;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.city FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.state_province_county = 'Georgia';

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, AVG(T1.price) FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T2.staff_id;

sql: SELECT T1.lesson_id, T1.price, T1.lesson_date FROM Lessons AS T1 JOIN Vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicle_id = T2.vehicle_id WHERE T2.vehicle_details = 'Car';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.amount_payment) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Customer_Payments AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T2.city = 'Port Melyssa' GROUP BY T1.customer_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.price) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.lesson_status_code != 'Cancelled' GROUP BY T1.staff_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.amount_payment FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.amount_payment = (SELECT MAX(amount_payment) FROM Customer_Payments);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.price) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Lessons AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 highest-spending customers and the total amount they have spent on lessons, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_status_code, SUM(T2.amount_payment) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total payment amount aggregated by each customer status code.

sql: SELECT AVG(lesson_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(lesson_id) AS lesson_count FROM Lessons GROUP BY customer_id) AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of lessons taken per customer across the entire dataset.

sql: SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m', datetime_payment) AS payment_month, SUM(amount_payment) AS total_payments FROM Customer_Payments GROUP BY payment_month ORDER BY total_payments DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T2.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Finished';

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T2.points) AS total_points FROM constructors AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY total_points DESC;

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, COUNT(T2.raceId) AS disqualifications FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T2.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Disqualified' GROUP BY T1.driverId;
