sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, COUNT(T2.stop) FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN pitStops AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T2.raceId = 841 GROUP BY T1.driverId;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing each driver's forename and surname along with the total count of their pit stops for a specific race (raceId 841).

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.position = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 80 rows listing the first and last names of drivers who have won a race but have never achieved a pole position in qualifying.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.resultId) AS fastest_laps FROM constructors AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId WHERE T2.rank = 1 GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY fastest_laps DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top constructors and their total count of fastest laps, ordered from the highest number of fastest laps to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T4.name, AVG(T1.milliseconds) FROM pitStops AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T2.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN constructors AS T4 ON T2.constructorId = T4.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'Malaysian Grand Prix' GROUP BY T4.name;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing each constructor team's name alongside their average pit stop time in milliseconds, specifically for the Malaysian Grand Prix.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, SUM(T2.points) AS total_points FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING SUM(T2.points) > (SELECT AVG(T4.points) FROM drivers AS T3 JOIN results AS T4 ON T3.driverId = T4.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'German');
result_summary: Returns 261 rows listing the full name and total career points for each driver whose total points exceed the average points per race scored by German drivers.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.forename, T3.surname, T2.grid FROM races AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T2.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN constructors AS T4 ON T2.constructorId = T4.constructorId WHERE T2.positionOrder = 1 AND T2.grid > 3 AND T4.name = 'McLaren';
result_summary: Returns 49 rows listing all race wins by McLaren drivers who started from 4th position or worse, showing the race name, driver's name, and their starting grid position for each win.

sql: SELECT T1.year, T3.forename, T3.surname, T2.wins FROM races AS T1 JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T2.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN (SELECT T4.year, MAX(T5.wins) AS max_wins FROM races AS T4 JOIN driverStandings AS T5 ON T4.raceId = T5.raceId WHERE T5.wins > 0 GROUP BY T4.year) AS T6 ON T1.year = T6.year AND T2.wins = T6.max_wins GROUP BY T1.year;
result_summary: Returns 68 rows, identifying one driver with the most race wins for each year from 1950 onwards, along with their full name and total win count for that season.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, COUNT(T2.raceId) AS race_count, SUM(T2.points) AS career_points FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING COUNT(T2.raceId) > 150 ORDER BY career_points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 37 rows listing experienced drivers who have competed in over 150 races, showing their total race count and career points, ordered from the highest total career points to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.year) AS years_hosted FROM circuits AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.circuitId = T2.circuitId GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.year) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 28 rows listing circuits that have hosted races in more than 10 different years, along with the total count of distinct years each circuit has hosted.

sql: SELECT T4.name, T2.forename, T2.surname, T3.forename, T3.surname FROM results AS T1_A JOIN results AS T1_B ON T1_A.raceId = T1_B.raceId AND T1_A.constructorId = T1_B.constructorId AND T1_A.driverId < T1_B.driverId JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1_A.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T1_B.driverId = T3.driverId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1_A.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T4.name = 'Australian Grand Prix';
result_summary: Returns 387 rows, listing each pair of drivers (by first and last name) who competed as teammates for the same constructor in the Australian Grand Prix.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId WHERE T1.positionOrder <= 5 GROUP BY T1.raceId, T1.constructorId HAVING COUNT(T1.driverId) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 48 rows listing the unique names of constructor teams that have had at least two of their drivers finish in the top 5 positions within the same race.

sql: SELECT T1.nationality, SUM(T2.laps) AS total_laps FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.nationality ORDER BY total_laps DESC;
result_summary: Returns 41 rows listing each driver nationality along with the total number of race laps completed by drivers from that country, ordered from highest to lowest total laps.

sql: SELECT T4.forename, T4.surname, T3.name, T2.position AS qualifying_pos, T1.positionOrder AS race_pos, (T2.position - T1.positionOrder) AS positions_gained FROM results AS T1 JOIN qualifying AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN drivers AS T4 ON T1.driverId = T4.driverId JOIN status AS T5 ON T1.statusId = T5.statusId WHERE T5.status = 'Finished' ORDER BY positions_gained DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the driver who gained the most positions from their qualifying start to their final finish in a single completed race, showing their name, the race details, and the number of positions gained.

sql: SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'British' UNION SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 211 rows listing the first name, last name, and nationality for all drivers who are either British or German.

sql: SELECT T2.year, AVG(T1.num_stops) AS avg_stops FROM (SELECT raceId, COUNT(stop) AS num_stops FROM pitStops GROUP BY raceId) AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId GROUP BY T2.year ORDER BY avg_stops DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single year with the highest average number of pit stops per race, along with that calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T2.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'McLaren' INTERSECT SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T2.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'Williams';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of drivers who have competed for both the McLaren and Williams constructor teams.

sql: SELECT T3.name, AVG(T1.fastestLapSpeed) AS avg_speed FROM results AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN circuits AS T3 ON T2.circuitId = T3.circuitId WHERE T1.fastestLapSpeed IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T3.name ORDER BY avg_speed DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three race circuits with the highest average fastest lap speeds, ordered from highest to lowest average speed.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.forename, T1.surname, T5.name FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN qualifying AS T3 ON T1.driverId = T3.driverId AND T2.raceId = T3.raceId JOIN lapTimes AS T4 ON T1.driverId = T4.driverId AND T2.raceId = T4.raceId JOIN races AS T5 ON T2.raceId = T5.raceId WHERE T2.positionOrder = 1 AND T3.position = 1 AND T2.rank = 1 AND T4.lap = 1 AND T4.position = 1;
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the drivers and the specific races where they achieved a dominant victory, defined as starting on pole, leading after the first lap, setting the fastest lap, and winning the race.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T2.year, SUM(T1.wins) AS season_wins FROM constructorStandings AS T1 JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId GROUP BY T3.name, T2.year ORDER BY season_wins DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the constructor with the most wins in a single season, showing the team's name, the year, and their total number of wins for that season.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, MIN(T3.date) AS first_race_date, MAX(T3.date) AS last_race_date FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T2.raceId = T3.raceId GROUP BY T1.driverId;
result_summary: Returns 840 rows, listing each driver by name along with the dates of their first and last recorded race.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.statusId = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.resultId) AS finish_percentage FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING COUNT(T2.resultId) >= 50 ORDER BY finish_percentage DESC;
result_summary: Returns 149 rows listing the race finish percentage for drivers with at least 50 race entries, ordered from the highest percentage to the lowest.
